commits.message:
feat(tui): prefix-based history search + show Ctrl+J in input hint (#3341)

* feat(tui): prefix-based history search + show Ctrl+J in input hint

When pressing Up with a partial text typed, only show history entries
that start with that prefix — matching the prompt-toolkit CLI behavior
described in #3311 ('no history/prefix matching like CLI').

Also updates both inline and normal-mode hint text from 'Alt+Enter for
newline' to 'Ctrl+J / Alt+Enter for newline', making the reliable
cross-terminal fallback visible to users.

Closes #3311

* fix(tui): key history edits by entry string to prevent cross-search leak

`_history_edits` was keyed by integer position within the current filter.
When the prefix changed, position 0 in the new filter was a different entry
than position 0 in the old filter — so an edit made during one prefix search
would appear verbatim when navigating the next.

Fix: key by the original entry string. Edits on "git status" persist across
Down/Up of the same search, but can never collide with "docker ps" at the
same index position.

Add `test_history_prefix_search_no_edit_leak` to guard against regression.

* test(tui): update snapshots for Ctrl+J hint

feat(tui): render thinking blocks as collapsibles, show Thinking… during generation (#3336)

* feat(tui): render thinking blocks as collapsibles, show Thinking… during generation

- Add _split_thinking() to parse <think>/<thinking> blocks from message content
- AssistantMessage now renders thinking blocks as collapsed Collapsibles with
  a muted italic style, so users can expand them to inspect reasoning
- StreamingMessage.set_thinking() updates the placeholder from "Generating…"
  to "Thinking…" when the model is inside a thinking block
- Thread on_thinking: Callable[[bool], None] through step() and reply() into
  _reply_stream(), which fires it at each are_thinking state transition
- GptmeApp._on_thinking() wires the callback to the stream widget and inline
  live area so both modes reflect the thinking/generating distinction
- CSS: .thinking-block Collapsible inherits muted color to visually distinguish
  reasoning from the main reply
- 9 new tests: _split_thinking variants, AssistantMessage collapsible rendering,
  StreamingMessage placeholder update/guard

Closes part of #3311 (thinking toggle / display item)

* fix(tui): preserve inline output across thinking transitions

test(tui): add SVG visual regression tests for TUI rendering (#3338)

* test(tui): add SVG visual regression tests for TUI rendering

Adds `tests/test_tui_visual.py` with six tests:

- **Gray background detection** (idle, stream, inline): assert no mid-gray
  Textual `$panel` color (#262626) appears in content-area rects. These
  tests document the regression from #3334 and are marked `xfail` until
  that fix merges (native_ansi_color=True on ActiveOutput).

- **Full SVG snapshots** (idle, stream, inline): compare the entire
  rendered SVG against baselines in `tests/snapshots/`. Catches palette
  and layout regressions beyond just background color.

Baselines are committed alongside the tests. To regenerate after an
intentional visual change:
    pytest tests/test_tui_visual.py --snapshot-update

On snapshot mismatch, the actual SVG is written as `*.actual.svg` next
to the baseline. CI uploads those as an artifact for easy inspection.

Closes #3335

* fix(test): normalize SVG whitespace in visual regression tests

Minify SVG snapshots by removing non-essential whitespace to handle
formatting differences between Textual versions. This makes snapshot
comparisons robust to indentation changes while preserving the actual
visual content (colors, positions, layout).

Regenerated baseline snapshots with minified format.

* fix(test): normalize status bar in TUI visual snapshots

The status bar shows "MODEL | Xk/Yk (Z%) | status" whose content and
background rect widths vary with the configured model name. CI uses
MODEL=local/test while local dev may use a different model, so any
run with a different model name caused all three snapshot tests to fail.

_normalize_svg now:
- Strips status bar text at any line (matched by the "Xk/Yk" pattern)
- Strips status bar background rects at y=562.7 (full mode, line-23)
  and y=172.3 (inline mode, line-7), both proportional to model name

Baselines regenerated from the CI actuals (MODEL=local/test) after
applying the new normalization, so they represent the stable visual
structure independent of the runtime model.

* fix(test): strip trailing newlines in SVG snapshot comparison

Pre-commit hooks add a trailing newline to committed SVG baseline files,
but textual's export_screenshot() produces SVGs without one. The byte-for-
byte comparison then fails despite identical visual content.

Strip trailing newlines from both sides before comparing so the test is
robust to this git/pre-commit convention mismatch.

* fix(test): regen TUI snapshots after #3334 merged to master

#3334 (native_ansi_color fix) changed some theme color values in the
Textual SVG output; baselines regenerated locally with MODEL=local/test
to match CI environment on the new master.

fix(tui): use terminal-native background for active output (#3334)

* fix(tui): use ansi_default background in inline mode to match terminal native bg

In inline mode (gptme -i), streamed content appears inside the Textual
screen with the theme's $background color (e.g. gray), while finalized
messages are printed to the terminal scrollback via _print_above() which
has no explicit background and naturally uses the terminal's native color.

This contrast caused a visible gray background during generation that
disappeared when the message was finalized — the bug Erik reported on
#3321.

Fix: apply background: ansi_default to Screen:inline and #live so the
streaming area uses the terminal's native background, matching the
finalized output path.

Adds a regression test verifying #live has ansi_default background in
inline mode.

* fix(tui): apply terminal background in both display modes

* fix(tui): preserve full-screen sizing

* fix(tui): use terminal-native ANSI theme

* fix(tui): preserve themed foreground colors

* fix(tui): apply terminal background to active surfaces

* fix(tui): preserve terminal default colors through output

fix(webui): persistent disconnected banner in conversation view (#3327)

* fix(webui): persistent disconnected banner in conversation view

When the server is unreachable, the app showed demo conversations in the
sidebar and let users navigate into them. The only sign of disconnection
was a disabled chat input with a small placeholder — easy to miss and
confusing to new users (issue #3326, also cited in the #2943 regression).

The WelcomeView already shows a clear alert when no conversation is open,
but once a user clicks a demo conversation the welcome screen disappears
and there was no indicator they were offline.

This adds a persistent amber banner at the top of ConversationContent that
fires when the API server is not connected and the user is not
intentionally in ?demo=1 mode. The banner:
- Is always visible (non-floating, takes up layout space)
- Shows the failure reason: CORS, Local Network Access, network refusal,
  timeout, or a generic "browsing demo data" fallback
- Has a Retry button that calls connect() and spins while retrying
- Disappears as soon as the connection is established

Intentional demo mode (?demo=1) is explicitly excluded — the banner only
fires for unintentional fallback-to-demo situations.

Also adds 7 focused unit tests covering the banner's show/hide logic and
each failure-reason path.

* fix(webui): banner reads correct server when serverId is provided

When ConversationContent renders a conversation that belongs to a
non-primary server (serverId prop set), the disconnection banner was
checking the *primary* server's isConnected$ and lastConnectionResult$,
and Retry was calling connect() which reconnects the primary.

Fix: use getClient(serverId) to get the conversation's actual server
client, read its observables for banner state/failure reason, and call
checkConnection() on it (not connect()) for Retry.

Adds 3 new unit tests covering the secondary-server scenario:
- banner hidden when secondary server connected (primary irrelevant)
- banner shown when secondary disconnected even if primary is connected
- Retry calls checkConnection() on secondary, not connect()

* fix(webui): detect removed server in registry, show explicit banner message

When a conversation's serverId is no longer registered (e.g. server was
removed from settings), getClient() silently falls back to the primary client.
The banner would then show the primary server's status, hiding the real
problem.

Fix: import getClientForServer directly and set serverNotFound=true when
the server ID is not in the registry. The banner now fires unconditionally
and shows "no longer registered" guidance rather than the primary server's
connection state.

Adds 2 unit tests covering the missing-server banner and demo-mode exclusion.

* fix(webui): hide Retry when server no longer in registry

When serverNotFound is true, getClient(serverId) falls back silently
to the primary client. Clicking Retry then called checkConnection() on
the primary rather than the missing server, leaving the banner stuck.

Since there is no registered server to retry, hide the Retry button in
this case — the banner message already tells the user to re-add the
server in settings.

Adds a unit test covering this scenario.

fix(webui): strip thinking blocks from TTS output (#3332)

* fix(webui): strip thinking blocks from TTS output

<thinking>/<think> tags contain model reasoning that should not be
read aloud. Add stripping in toSpokenText() so both auto-play on
message completion and the manual 'Read aloud' button skip them.

Also add three tests covering: <thinking>, <think>, and thinking-only
messages (where nothing should be spoken).

Closes #3330

* fix(webui): omit tool-use blocks from TTS

* fix(webui): strip xml and @tool-format tool calls from TTS

Add xml-format (<tool-use>...</tool-use>) and @tool-format (@name: {json}
or @name(id): {json}) stripping to toSpokenText(), alongside the existing
markdown-format (fenced block) stripping. All three are gptme tool-call
formats (ToolFormat in gptme/tools/base.py) and must never be spoken.

Adds four new tests covering xml-only-silent, xml-mixed, @tool-mixed,
and @tool-with-id-mixed cases.

* fix(webui): silence interrupted thinking blocks

* fix(webui): distinguish interrupted reasoning from examples

feat(tui): tab-completion candidate overlay (#3311) (#3316)

* feat(tui): add tab-completion candidate overlay (#3311)

When the user presses Tab on a slash-command prefix that has multiple
completions, show a small overlay list above the input with the candidates.
The currently-selected entry is highlighted with a ▶ prefix. Repeated Tab
presses cycle through the list. Any non-Tab key (including Enter) hides
the overlay.

Implementation:
- ChatInput.CompletionsChanged message posted whenever the candidate list
  changes (set, cycled, or cleared)
- ChatInput._clear_completions() helper clears state and posts an empty
  CompletionsChanged so the overlay disappears
- GptmeApp.on_chat_input_completions_changed updates a #completions Static
  widget (display: none by default) above the input; >1 candidate = show,
  else hide
- Clears on Enter, Alt+Enter/Ctrl+J, and any non-Tab key press

Tests (4 new, 22 total pass):
- overlay appears with multiple candidates
- overlay hides on non-Tab key
- overlay hides on Enter
- Tab cycling updates overlay selection

* fix(tui): keep active completion visible

* fix(tui): clamp completion overlay start to 0 for short candidate lists

When len(candidates) < max_visible (2-7 items), the inner min() was
returning a negative start. enumerate(visible, start=negative) then
assigned negative indices that never matched the nonnegative selected
index, so no selection marker was rendered.

Fix: wrap with max(0, ...) so start is always >= 0.
Add regression test: 3-candidate list with last item selected.

docs(server): add Security section — threat model, auth model, and workspace PATCH semantics (#3333)

* docs(server): add Security section — threat model, auth, workspace PATCH semantics

Settles the design questions raised in #3320:
- Authentication model: loopback (no auth) vs network (bearer token) vs GPTME_DISABLE_AUTH
- Threat model: CSRF is blocked by CORS preflight; DNS rebinding by Host-header
  validation (already implemented in #3324)
- What workspace containment does NOT address: authorized clients can already
  execute arbitrary shell commands, so restricting the creation workspace adds no
  security boundary (documented in the PUT handler; now in user-facing docs too)
- Workspace PATCH semantics: create-any, re-target-never asymmetry explained and
  justified as a confused-deputy guard

Refs #3320

* docs(server): fix auth coverage claim and add data-loss warning for workspace change

- Qualify 'every API request' to note that version, config, metrics and
  API docs routes remain unauthenticated on network binds
- Add explicit destructive-operation warning to the delete-and-recreate
  workspace change procedure

Addresses Greptile P1/P2 review findings on #3333.

* docs(server): add /api/v2 root to public-route inventory

The API root itself is unauthenticated on network binds; add it to the
list alongside version, config, metrics and docs routes.

fix(test): kill TestMaxTimeWatchdog xdist flake — prevent untracked threads (#3331)

* fix(test): kill TestMaxTimeWatchdog xdist flake — untracked thread + group isolation

Two tests in TestMaxTimeWatchdog manually removed their subagent from _subagents
before the autouse cleanup_subagents_after fixture could see it.  If the
run_subagent thread was still alive at that point (e.g. mid lazy-import of
gptme.logmanager), it became untracked and raced the next test's setup phase
with sys.modules mutations, producing:
  RuntimeError: dictionary changed size during iteration

Fix (class-level, not per-site):
- Remove the premature _subagents[:] = [...] slices from the two watchdog-launch
  tests; cleanup_subagents_after is the authoritative teardown path (PR #3257).
- Add assert not sa.thread.is_alive() after the join so an unexpected thread-still-alive
  condition turns into a clear AssertionError rather than a later obscure flake.
- Add @pytest.mark.xdist_group("max_time_watchdog") to the class so all nine
  tests run sequentially on the same xdist worker, preventing cross-class
  scheduler interleaving from triggering the race.

Fixes #3328.

* fix(test): remove ineffective xdist group marker

feat(tui): tool-execution placeholder in chat + fix gray message background (#3321)

* feat(tui): add tool-execution placeholder in chat + fix gray message background

- Add ToolPlaceholder widget shown in the chat area while a tool is
  executing (between assistant message and tool output arriving), mirroring
  the existing StreamingMessage pattern for generation
- Show 'Running tool...' in the #live area during tool execution in inline mode
- Clear the placeholder when tool output arrives or generation ends
- Fix the gray background on message containers: .message was missing
  background: transparent, so Textual's surface color bled through the
  container even after the earlier Static-only fix (#3312)
- Add test covering placeholder show/clear lifecycle

* fix(tui): tool placeholder lifecycle — skip tool-free responses, track multi-tool count

Two bugs in the ToolPlaceholder introduced in the previous commit:

1. Placeholder appeared for every assistant message, even tool-free plain-text
   replies. Fix: only show it when the message contains ≥1 runnable tool call.

2. Placeholder cleared on the first system (tool output) message even when more
   tools from the same response were still executing. Fix: track the expected
   tool count and decrement on each system message; clear only when count hits 0.

Adds two regression tests:
- test_tool_placeholder_not_shown_for_tool_free_response
- test_tool_placeholder_persists_across_multiple_tools

* fix(tui): end tool placeholder on step boundary

* fix(tui): style progress placeholders without Rich dim

test(server): regression test for generation_complete ordering (refs #3315) (#3323)

* test(server): regression test for generation_complete ordering (refs #3315)

Adds a deadlock-based regression test to verify that generation_complete is
emitted before _try_auto_name_and_notify runs (#2081 fix).

The test patches auto-naming to block on a gate that opens only after
generation_complete is received by the SSE client. If the regression recurs
(auto-naming called before generation_complete), the gate never opens,
wait_for_event times out, and the test fails. With the correct ordering,
the gate opens immediately and the test passes.

* test(server): synchronize SSE regression teardown

fix(webui): omit default dot-workspace so server applies its @log default (#3329)

When no workspace is explicitly chosen, the webui was always forwarding
`workspace: '.'` to the server.  After PR #3319, the server now defaults
to an isolated `@log` workspace per-conversation when none is provided —
but that default was never reached because the webui always filled in `'.'`.

Introduce `requestWorkspace()`:
- returns `undefined` for unset / `'.'` → key is omitted from JSON, server
  picks `@log` (isolated per-conversation directory)
- passes explicit non-default paths through unchanged (e.g. `/workspace`
  from the workspace picker, or cloud pod absolute paths)

Update the placeholder `initConversation` workspace to `'@log'` when no
explicit workspace was supplied (the concrete resolved path arrives via the
first SSE event; `@log` is an accurate placeholder label).

Fixes part of #3320.

fix(server): validate Host header to block DNS-rebinding on unauthenticated local servers (#3324)

* fix(server): validate Host header to block DNS-rebinding on unauthenticated local servers

gptme-server disables bearer auth for loopback binds. CORS preflight blocks
plain cross-origin JSON, but DNS rebinding defeats CORS entirely: a malicious
site whose hostname re-resolves to 127.0.0.1 becomes same-origin with the local
server, giving a drive-by page full unauthenticated API access — which includes
shell execution via the agent.

Validate the request Host header (before_request hook) when auth is disabled for
a loopback bind. localhost/127.0.0.1/[::1] (any port) are always allowed; a
concrete bind host and --allowed-hosts entries extend the allow-list for proxied
setups. Disallowed hosts get a 403 JSON error naming the host and how to allow
it.

Validation is skipped when auth is enabled (bearer token already gates access)
and when GPTME_DISABLE_AUTH is explicitly set without an allow-list (cloud pods
bind 0.0.0.0 behind an authenticated ingress; the operator owns security) — but
--allowed-hosts is honored even under GPTME_DISABLE_AUTH.

Part of #3320.

* fix(server): accept trailing-dot FQDN form of allowed hosts

Host: localhost. (the absolute-DNS root form) is a valid, loopback-resolving
host but was rejected with 403 because the parser kept the trailing dot while
the allow-list holds only "localhost". Strip a trailing root dot in
_extract_hostname so fully-qualified equivalents of an allowed host match.

Addresses Greptile P1 on #3324.

* fix(server): normalize allow-list hosts to match Host-header parsing

An operator configuring an absolute hostname (e.g. --allowed-hosts gptme.local.)
kept the trailing root dot in the allow-list while _extract_hostname strips it
from incoming Host headers, so the explicitly allowed request got a 403. Route
allow-list entries (--allowed-hosts and the bind host) through the same
normalization via a shared _normalize_allowed_host helper.

Addresses second Greptile P1 on #3324.

fix(server): allow explicit workspace override when creating conversations (#3319)

* fix(server): allow explicit workspace override when creating conversations

PR #2943 added a workspace containment check to the conversation create
endpoint (PUT /api/v2/conversations/<id>), rejecting any workspace outside
the conversation's logdir. This broke conversation creation from the webui
entirely: the webui sends an explicit workspace ('.' by default, or an
absolute path from the workspace picker) with every create request, so
every new webui conversation failed with 400
'workspace escapes conversation logdir'.

(gptme-cloud prod/staging were unaffected only because their pinned
gptme-server image predates #2943.)

The containment check adds no security boundary at creation time: an
authorized API client can already execute arbitrary shell commands through
the agent. The real boundaries are bearer auth (required for network
binds) and CORS preflight (cross-origin JSON PUTs are blocked unless
--cors-origin is passed). Restore the documented client override at
creation, keeping the @log isolated default when no workspace is given.

Workspace containment on the config PATCH endpoint (mutating existing
conversations) is kept as-is.

* test(webui): add e2e regression test for conversation creation

The e2e suite connected, listed and opened existing conversations, but
never created one through the UI — which is how the workspace-containment
regression (create 400s from the webui) went unnoticed. This test types a
message, submits, and asserts navigation to the new conversation with the
message rendered.

* chore: retrigger CI

* fix(server): allow config PATCH to round-trip persisted external workspace

The webui settings dialog echoes the full config (including workspace) on
every save, so the PATCH containment check rejected any settings change on
conversations with external workspaces (CLI-created, or created via PUT
with an explicit workspace). Only reject when the PATCH actually changes
the workspace to a path outside the logdir. (Greptile finding on #3319)

* fix(ci): make webui e2e actually test the server under review

Two defects made the webui e2e suite blind to server regressions:

1. CORS origin mismatch: Playwright serves the webui at
   http://127.0.0.1:5701 but the server allowed only http://localhost:5701
   (different origin for CORS). The webui silently ran offline against demo
   data, so no e2e test ever exercised the API server started in CI.
2. The 'dev' pass installed gptme from git master instead of the PR
   checkout, so even with CORS fixed, server changes under review were
   never tested pre-merge.

Also gate the new conversation-creation test off the stable pass until a
release ships the #3319 fix (latest PyPI release has the #2943 create
regression), and wait for the connected/enabled state before typing.

* test(webui): hard-fail e2e when disconnected; fix keyboard tests for connected state

Add a non-negotiable connectivity test: the suite previously passed in
full while silently running offline against demo data (the dangerous
failure mode that hid server regressions). Now a dedicated test fails
loudly when the app can't reach the API server.

The keyboard shortcut tests relied on that offline state: with a live
connection the chat input is enabled and autofocused, so '?' typed into
the input instead of opening the shortcuts dialog. Blur the input first.

fix(server,webui): external sessions dedup, stable detail IDs, admin nav label (#3325)

Three fixes for the external-sessions integration and sidebar:

- Exclude gptme sessions under this server's own logs dir from external
  discovery: they are already served as native conversations, so the Ext
  toggle showed every local chat twice (native + 'external-extracted').
- Derive catalog IDs from the discovered path instead of the transcript's
  trajectory_path. The transcript may report a differently-normalized path
  (e.g. ~/.claude symlinked to ~/dotfiles/home/.claude), which made every
  listed session 404 on GET /api/v2/external-sessions/<id> since the
  detail lookup hashes the discover path. Canonicalize both.
- CLI provider: normalize gptme session dirs to conversation.jsonl before
  invoking 'gptme-sessions transcript' (it rejects directories with a
  usage error, spamming warnings for every gptme session).
- webui: render the Admin nav entry through the same navItems path as the
  other sections so it gets a label when expanded and an aria-label
  (was a bare icon button).

feat(tui): persist input history to shared prompt-toolkit history file (#3314)

* feat(tui): persist input history to shared prompt-toolkit history file

TUI now loads history from and writes to the same FileHistory file used
by the CLI (~/.local/share/gptme/history.pt), so sessions share history
across invocations and between TUI and CLI.

- _load_pt_history(): parses the prompt-toolkit file format (oldest first)
- _append_pt_history(): appends one entry in the same format store_string() uses
- ChatInput.__init__: loads history from file on startup
- ChatInput._push_history: appends new entries to the file

Adds three tests: roundtrip, missing-file, and cross-session persistence.

Fixes part of gptme/gptme#3311

* fix(tui): isolate history write errors and make appends atomic

Two P1 issues from Greptile:

1. A write failure in _append_pt_history (e.g. read-only fs, full disk)
   would propagate through _push_history and abort on_chat_input_submitted
   before the prompt was enqueued — silently dropping the user's input.
   Wrap the call in try/except OSError with a warning log.

2. The prior implementation made two or more separate f.write() calls per
   entry (timestamp line + one line per content line). Concurrent CLI and TUI
   sessions appending simultaneously could interleave those writes, producing
   corrupted entries on the next load. Fix: build the full entry string first,
   then write it under fcntl.LOCK_EX so the append is atomic.

Add two tests covering the new guarantees:
- test_pt_history_write_error_is_isolated
- test_pt_history_concurrent_appends

* fix(tui): share portable lock across history writers

fix(envs): set LiberoEnvConfig.fps default to 20 to match robosuite (#4124)

* fix(envs): set LiberoEnvConfig.fps default to 20 to match robosuite

LiberoEnvConfig.fps was set to 30, but the underlying robosuite
OffScreenRenderEnv always runs at its default control_freq of 20 Hz
since fps is never passed through. This mismatch silently decouples
the dataset/eval loop rate from the actual simulation step rate.

Set the default to 20 to match the real sim rate and avoid the
footgun.

Fixes #3368

* fix(libero): apply configured control frequency

---------

Co-authored-by: xinmotlanthua <275663218+xinmotlanthua@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: tom-doerr <tomdoerr96@gmail.com>

fix(openarms): stop set_zero_position()ing on connect (#4058)

* fix(damiao): make is_calibrated a plain property, not cached

`is_calibrated` was a `@cached_property`, so it froze at its first-read
value and never reflected later changes to `self.calibration` (set by
connect/calibrate/load). This caused the OpenArm teleop to re-run
calibration even when a calibration file existed, and to skip
`set_zero_position()` after a fresh calibration.

Switch to `@property` (matching the MotorsBus base contract and the
Feetech/SO-100 buses) and drop the now-unused `functools.cached_property`
import.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

feat(diffusion): add gradient checkpointing for memory optimization (#4127)

* feat(diffusion): add gradient checkpointing for memory optimization

Add gradient_checkpointing config option to DiffusionPolicy. When
enabled, wraps the UNet encoder, mid, and decoder residual blocks
with torch.utils.checkpoint.checkpoint to trade compute for memory.

Allows training with larger batch sizes or higher-resolution inputs
on memory-constrained GPUs. Disabled by default.

Usage: --policy.gradient_checkpointing=true

Part of the 0.6.0 roadmap item 3.3 (gradient checkpointing for all
policies).

* test(diffusion): verify gradient checkpointing parity

feat(annotate): run lerobot-annotate on HF Jobs via --job.target (#4095)

* feat(annotate): run lerobot-annotate on HF Jobs via --job.target

Annotation needed a hand-edited launcher script (examples/annotations/run_hf_job.py)
to reach a GPU: users copied it, rewrote the embedded CMD string for their dataset,
and ran it with `python`. Fold that into the CLI instead, mirroring `lerobot-train`:
`lerobot-annotate --job.target=h200` submits the exact command you'd run locally.

- AnnotationJobConfig extends JobConfig with the annotation runtime's defaults
  (vllm/vllm-openai image, 2h cap) plus --job.lerobot_ref, so an unmerged branch
  can be exercised remotely without editing a script.
- lerobot.jobs.annotate builds the pod command by replaying the user's own CLI
  flags (minus --job.*/--root, with --repo_id re-emitted from the config) after a
  setup prelude that installs lerobot on top of the vLLM image. Job monitoring,
  log tailing and Ctrl-C-detaches reuse the training submitter's plumbing.
- Remote runs require --repo_id; a local-only dataset is pushed privately first.

The generated pod command is byte-for-byte the script's old CMD.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(annotate): reject client-side config files on remote runs

draccus exposes `--config_path` plus a `--<field>` config-file arg for every
nested dataclass (`--vlm`, `--plan`, `--job`, ...). All name files on the
client's disk, so forwarding them to the pod silently dropped whatever settings
they carried. Reject them up front instead.

Bare `--job` also slipped past the `--job.` prefix filter, so a `--job=cfg.yaml`
holding `target: h200` would have reached the pod and had the job submit a job
of its own, recursively. It is dropped from the forwarded args as well.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(jobs): share the submit-and-follow loop between both submitters

`submit_annotate_to_hf` reused the leaf helpers (`_poll_until_done`, `_tail_logs`,
`_pod_forwarded_args`) but duplicated the orchestration around them: ~40 of the 50
lines that spawn the poll/log threads, install the Ctrl-C-detaches handler and
raise on a non-COMPLETED stage were identical in both files.

Extract that into `follow_job(job_id, *, detach, success_marker=None) -> bool`,
returning True when the job finished and False when we stopped watching without a
verdict (detach or Ctrl-C). Training keeps its model-pushed marker by passing it in;
annotation has no equivalent line (the CLI keeps working after the upload log to
write the card and tag) so its completion stays stage-based.

Kept in hf.py rather than a new module so every existing monkeypatch target in
test_hf.py still resolves.

Behaviour change: a training run whose job reaches COMPLETED without the marker
matching now prints its completion line instead of returning silently. The marker
was already documented as an optimisation with a stage-based fallback; the fallback
just never reported success.

Tests: adds annotate coverage for the non-detach path (completion and failure) —
previously only ever exercised with detach=true — plus a detach short-circuit test.
Both new annotate tests verified to fail under a mutation that stubs out follow_job.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.message:
Use the default app when no bundle id is specified for `app` context

Summary:
### This diff

* Updates `idb-repl` to accept an `app` context with no bundle id.
* Adds app installation for the default `ReplHost` app when no other app is specified for the `app` context.

Reviewed By: bryanyantx

Differential Revision: D113353092

fbshipit-source-id: ae99adf16097576cca97f1968576458e42e7df64

Add the default ReplHost app

Summary:
### This diff

Adds an empty application to the `Resources/` that can be used as the default app for code injection in repl sessions.

Reviewed By: bryanyantx

Differential Revision: D113305085

fbshipit-source-id: e3ee400dfeab10bbda459faaf51c87bac56e92c4

Enforce TLS 1.2 minimum for Portal Companion Local Discovery

Summary:
The Portal Companion Local Discovery TLS endpoints in the idb `CompanionServer` module built their NIOSSL `TLSConfiguration` without pinning a minimum protocol version. NIOSSL defaults `minimumTLSVersion` to `.tlsv1` (TLS 1.0), which leaves the deprecated TLS 1.0/1.1 protocols — and their legacy CBC/3DES/RSA cipher suites — negotiable.

This pins `configuration.minimumTLSVersion = .tlsv12` in both the server (`makeServerSSLContext()` in `CompanionServer.swift`) and client (`makeClientSSLContext()` in `CompanionClient.swift`) SSL context builders. With a TLS 1.2 floor, TLS 1.0/1.1 can no longer be negotiated and the deprecated cipher suites tied to those protocols are excluded; NIOSSL/BoringSSL selects only modern TLS 1.2+ suites.

Addresses the deprecated transport-layer protocol and weak cipher suite findings for the Portal Companion Local Discovery workflow.

Reviewed By: chrisjhur

Differential Revision: D113001869

fbshipit-source-id: 9da70bc5df7978c6f28759d818fc9f7afccc9bf2

Add ui describe by accessibility marker

Summary:
Adds `idb ui describe <marker>`, which resolves the accessibility element whose value contains the marker and prints its serialized subtree. `--match-key` selects the searchable key (AXLabel default), `--depth` bounds the search, and `--nested` selects the nested format.

Rather than a new RPC, this extends the existing `accessibility_info` read: `AccessibilityInfoRequest` gains an optional marker target (plus match key and depth), and the companion resolves and serializes it via the shared `FBSimulatorControl` describe path. `describe-all` (whole screen) and `describe-point` (point) are unchanged; the Python client's `accessibility_info` gains the marker target and the `describe` CLI command routes through it.

Reviewed By: xgerrit

Differential Revision: D112597919

fbshipit-source-id: 46c46e57dba2b07e8692098907604583b6091630

Support accessibility tap by marker, point, key and expected value

Summary:
Extends `idb ui tap` beyond coordinate HID taps with a first-class accessibility (AXPress) path:

- `idb ui tap <x> <y> --api ax` resolves the accessibility element at the point and presses it instead of sending a coordinate touch.
- `idb ui tap <marker>` resolves an element whose accessibility value contains the marker and presses it. `--match-key` selects the searchable key (AXLabel default, plus AXUniqueId/AXValue/title/role/...), `--depth` bounds the tree search, and `--expected-value`/`--expected-key` assert the element's value before pressing.

`idb ui tap <x> <y>` (coordinate HID tap) is unchanged. Introduces the `accessibility_action` RPC: its request carries a marker-or-point target, match key and depth, plus an `action` oneof whose `Tap` variant holds the optional expected value/key. The companion resolves the target and presses via the shared `FBSimulatorControl` accessibility path, and the Python client gains `accessibility_tap`.

Reviewed By: xgerrit

Differential Revision: D112597920

fbshipit-source-id: 4e0f8be21b821c2b727eaf9502048f4b38e7db92

Model the accessibility target as a sum type

Summary:
Introduce `AccessibilityTarget` — the type used to select an accessibility element — and thread it through the `Client` API instead of parallel positional/optional arguments. This starts with `accessibility_info`: its `point` argument becomes `target: AccessibilityTarget | None` (currently just `AccessibilityPoint`, or `None` for the whole screen). The marker-based accessibility commands added later in the stack extend this same union rather than adding more parallel arguments.

No behavioral change: `ui describe-all` / `ui describe-point` resolve their target exactly as before, and the gRPC wire is unchanged.

Differential Revision: D113394110

fbshipit-source-id: 9d8253cbdb377358b40bc353c34ac9cabb4d5c6d

Add autofill-passwords simulator setting

Summary:
Adds an `autofill-passwords` setting to disable iOS AutoFill Passwords on a simulator, exposed through both `idb set autofill-passwords disable` and `sime2e set autofill-passwords disable`. It writes `com.apple.WebUI` / `AutoFillPasswords`, which suppresses the native "Automatic Strong Password" cover shown over `UITextContentTypeNewPassword` fields (and QuickType password fill) for apps launched afterwards; no reboot is required.

Implemented as a single `FBSimulatorSetting.autoFillPasswords` case, so both CLIs pick it up through the shared parser and `apply` with no per-tool code.

Reviewed By: nganbread

Differential Revision: D113399891

fbshipit-source-id: 70cb295fb1967a515ab56771b205234819e7051f

Resolve curated setting names via the shared registry

Summary: `idb set <name> <value>` now resolves curated setting names (appearance, content-size, and the slow-animations / increase-contrast toggles) through the shared `FBSimulatorSetting` parser in FBSimulatorControl. These settings were previously reachable only from sime2e; they now work through idb with no per-setting proto message or Python branch. Any non-curated name still falls back to a raw preference write, so `idb set <key> <value> --domain <domain>` is unchanged.

Reviewed By: nganbread

Differential Revision: D113399887

fbshipit-source-id: ff160a000f9813b8e56fa94844ea60294c4be991

Flatten simulator settings into one enum and apply API

Summary:
No behavioral change. Replaces the flat `FBSimulatorSetting` toggle enum  with a single associated-value `FBSimulatorSetting` enum in FBSimulatorControl, and collapses the per-setting setter methods (`setSetting`/`setAppearance`/`setContentSizeCategory`/`setPreference`) on `SettingsCommands` into a single `apply(_:)` entry point.

`FBSimulatorSetting(name:value:type:domain:)` centralizes the `set`-command name parsing that previously lived only in sime2e's `asSetting`, so idb and sime2e share one source of truth for setting names and their value grammar. `FBSimulatorAppearance` and `FBSimulatorContentSizeCategory` drop vestigial `objc` (no Objective-C consumers) while keeping their `Int` raw values for the CoreSimulator bridge.

Reviewed By: nganbread

Differential Revision: D113399892

fbshipit-source-id: 689e343de33d735e88b7c2fdf1f4b49eea05b916

Characterize the set command preference forwarding

Summary: No behavioral change. Adds unit tests characterizing the existing `idb set` command: an arbitrary `name`/`value` (with optional `--type`/`--domain`) is forwarded to the client's `set_preference`, apart from the `locale`/`hardware-keyboard` special cases. Covers a plain name, a domain-scoped write, and the `autofill-passwords` name.

Reviewed By: nganbread

Differential Revision: D113400754

fbshipit-source-id: fbb5d0b1d8db5981da747976048ce99fdc400329

Log per-subcommand attribution to CLI telemetry

Summary:
idb now resolves the invoked subcommand path of each invocation — e.g. `idb ui describe-all` -> `["ui", "describe-all"]`, `idb launch` -> `["launch"]` — and passes it to usage-logging plugins through the `on_launch` plugin hook, so usage can be attributed per subcommand rather than only recording that idb ran.

`CompositeCommand.resolve_subcommand_path` walks the argparse command tree exactly like command dispatch does, normalising aliased invocations to their canonical command name. Leaf commands with no chosen subcommand resolve to an empty path.

Reviewed By: xgerrit

Differential Revision: D113389263

fbshipit-source-id: 27fa6f46ca1ea9cc12a09afd3b4b23dd0c328ea0

Add ui shake gesture

Summary: Adds `idb ui shake`, which shakes the device. It rides the existing `hid` streaming RPC: an empty `HIDShake` member is added to the `HIDEvent` oneof, the companion maps it to the `FBSimulatorControl` shake HID event, and the Python client gains a `shake` method plus the `ui shake` CLI command.

Reviewed By: xgerrit

Differential Revision: D113216217

fbshipit-source-id: c8f0f2767f3f412bfd0e3504c4496a480e40bcfb

Add ui rotate gesture

Summary: Adds `idb ui rotate <ORIENTATION>`, which sets the device orientation (PORTRAIT, PORTRAIT_UPSIDE_DOWN, LANDSCAPE_LEFT, LANDSCAPE_RIGHT). It rides the existing `hid` streaming RPC: a `HIDOrientation` member is added to the `HIDEvent` oneof, the companion maps it to the `FBSimulatorControl` device-orientation HID event, and the Python client gains a `rotate` method plus the `ui rotate` CLI command.

Reviewed By: xgerrit

Add a Swift Package Manager build for `idb-repl`

### This diff

Update nearly all targets to build with Swift 6

### Context

fbshipit-source-id: ed794cba461e98d49ab754c9c950a2178dc87df2

Add open-source sync agent rule for fbcode/idb

Summary: Adds an agent rule under `fbcode/idb/.llms/rules/` recording that `fbcode/idb/` is mirrored to public GitHub, so contributors and AI agents keep Meta-internal code out of the synced source files and keep internal details out of published commit-message titles and summaries. This mirrors the equivalent open-source-sync guidance that already exists for the idb companion.

Differential Revision: D113391826

fbshipit-source-id: 5cc618d47710dbdda7b065cc150c7506cbb6ff9f

Model MPEG-TS packet writing

Summary: Introduce small MPEG-TS packet helpers for section packets and PES payload packetization. PAT/PMT construction now computes and patches `section_length` and CRC through one section writer, and video plus timed-metadata PES packetization share the same adaptation-field stuffing logic.

Reviewed By: xgerrit

Differential Revision: D112996054

fbshipit-source-id: 6db3df34515b7a4e1dcb6fbfd31ab253f2ab40c0

Model fMP4 boxes with a writer

Summary: Replace the fMP4 primitive byte-writing free functions with `FBFMP4BoxWriter`, a small value that owns the output bytes and exposes big-endian primitive writes plus `beginBox`/`endBox` operations. This keeps the box-size patching and buffer mutation on one type instead of passing `inout [UInt8]` through every fMP4 helper.

Reviewed By: xgerrit

Differential Revision: D112995389

fbshipit-source-id: 4f4dd29baeef030d872c2f5b27bf924d41ce2e2f

Type fMP4 codec helpers

Summary: Pass `FBVideoStreamCodec` through the fMP4 helper layer instead of deriving and threading an `isHEVC` boolean. The fMP4 compatible brand, sample entry type, codec config atom type, and fallback parameter-set builder are now selected from the codec enum.

Reviewed By: xgerrit

Differential Revision: D112994539

fbshipit-source-id: fe3c2092ea35952bb9c2d076b99e133ddf966996

Add shared accessibility set-value

Summary:
Adds `accessibilitySetValue(for:value:)` on `AccessibilityOperations`, resolving an
`FBAccessibilityElementQuery` and setting the element's accessibility value, always
closing the element.

This consolidates the resolve-then-set-value logic that callers previously
reimplemented on top of `FBSimulatorControl` into one shared path, so every
front-end sets values identically.

Behaviour is unchanged: sime2e's `ui set-value` now routes through the shared path.

Reviewed By: xgerrit

Differential Revision: D113216215

fbshipit-source-id: c0388db29edd2d8946efce428f5d2b365fadc927

Add shared accessibility scroll

Summary:
Adds `accessibilityScroll(for:direction:)` on `AccessibilityOperations`, resolving
an `FBAccessibilityElementQuery` and scrolling the element in the given direction,
always closing the element.

This consolidates the resolve-then-scroll logic that callers previously
reimplemented on top of `FBSimulatorControl` into one shared path, so every
front-end scrolls identically.

Behaviour is unchanged: sime2e's `ui scroll` now routes through the shared path
(the point / marker / frontmost target resolution is preserved).

Reviewed By: xgerrit

Differential Revision: D113216218

fbshipit-source-id: b5035d915786343871920229368fd776e4bf3ef3

Add shared accessibility tap

Summary:
Adds `accessibilityTap(for:expectedValue:expectedKey:)` on
`AccessibilityOperations`, resolving an `FBAccessibilityElementQuery` and
performing an AXPress with optional value verification, always closing the
element. Adds `FBAccessibilityExpectedValueMismatch` for the verification
failure.

This consolidates the resolve-then-tap logic that callers previously
reimplemented on top of `FBSimulatorControl` into one shared path, so every
front-end taps identically.

Behaviour is unchanged on all success paths. The one surface change: a tap
`--expected-value` mismatch now throws `FBAccessibilityExpectedValueMismatch`
(same message text) instead of an argument-parser validation error, changing
that rare error path's exit code from 64 to 1.

Reviewed By: xgerrit

Differential Revision: D112597922

fbshipit-source-id: 0c9953bbc14331cfcee40ba3d11a7fb3c0991a83

Add shared accessibility describe and serialize

Summary:
No behavioral change. Adds `accessibilityDescribe(for:options:)` on
`AccessibilityOperations`, which resolves an `FBAccessibilityElementQuery` and
serializes the element to canonical sorted-keys JSON, always closing the element.

This consolidates the resolve-then-serialize describe path (frontmost, at-point,
and by-marker) that callers previously each assembled on top of
`FBSimulatorControl` into one shared implementation, so every front-end emits an
identical serialization.

Reviewed By: xgerrit

Differential Revision: D112597928

fbshipit-source-id: 069c4cc3325699c9733f7f60030d6181a4a3a46a

Add shared accessibility element query and resolver

Summary:
No behavioral change. Introduces `FBAccessibilityElementQuery` — a resolvable
reference to an accessibility element by screen point, by marker (matched against
a searchable key up to a given depth), or the frontmost application — and an
`accessibilityElement(for:)` resolver on `AccessibilityOperations` that
dispatches to the existing point / matching / frontmost primitives.

This consolidates the point-or-marker element-resolution logic that callers
previously reimplemented on top of `FBSimulatorControl` into one shared path, so
every front-end resolves elements identically.

Reviewed By: xgerrit

Differential Revision: D112597918

Add session replays

It's super useful to be able to replay exactly what happened in a repl session.

commits.message:
Make the keyboard smaller on modern phones (#1367)

The keyboard height was proportional to the height of the screen. This
was when the most popular ratio was 16:9. The formula is changed to work
with modern phones that have a ratio of 19:9 or bigger.

The option in the settings is no longer a % of the height of the screen
but a % of an arbitrary unit.

commits.message:
feat(data-api): add data change notification capability

Add the post-commit main-to-renderer data change notification
infrastructure frozen in the Phase A contract (#17144):

- DataApiDataChangeEffect discriminated union (scalar / projection /
  membership / order) with GetMethodApiPaths derived from GET response
  shapes; illegal states are unrepresentable and the collection
  classification is pinned by a snapshot type test
- notifyDataApiDataChange broadcasts over the single fixed
  DataApi_DataChanged channel behind the application.isReady() delivery
  boundary; failures never reach the committed write path
- renderer DataApiService fan-out (exact endpoint match, one merged
  callback per notification, per-listener error isolation) attached at
  construction, plus the useDataChange hook
- replace the never-shipped subscribe placeholder (IPC channels,
  IpcAdapter handlers, preload subscribe, Subscription types) and drop
  the deprecated zero-consumer cancelRequest / cancelAllRequests /
  getRequestStats
- mocks mirror production semantics through the service mock's single
  fan-out, pinned by a mock-parity test; docs cover governance fences
  and consumer usage

fix(window-manager): isolate per-window send failures in broadcast

Previously one window's webContents.send failure aborted the loop,
skipping the remaining windows and throwing into the caller; each
window is now isolated with a warn log.

Also align the unified application mock with real ServiceContainer
semantics: getOptional() throws for non-conditional services and
returns undefined for unknown names.

docs(claude-md): prune outdated sections and compress low-frequency guidance

Remove the BinaryManager section, the completed Data Layer / UI Layer migration items, and the research-via-subagent rule.
Rewrite the v1-residue posture without naming the removed legacy stacks and correct the shared type placement rule.
Compress the migration merge-conflict and data-classify sections in place, keeping all operative rules.
Document pnpm test:lint as the CI-equivalent lint gate.

feat(ai-reasoning): data-driven reasoning controls from the provider registry (#17034)

> ### 🚨 Branch strategy — read before opening this PR
>
> The v2 refactor has merged into `main`, so **`main` is the default
branch for active development** (v1 and v2 code currently coexist there
— expect large, breaking changes).
>
> - **Active development** (features, refactors, optimizations, fixes
for the current codebase) → target **`main`** (the default base).
> - **v1 maintenance** (hotfixes and subsequent v1 releases) → branch
from and target **`v1`**, _not_ `main`.
>
> A v1 fix does **not** auto-carry to `main`: if the same bug exists on
`main`, open a separate forward-port PR targeting `main`. Before
touching subsystems being replaced, read `docs/references/data/` and
watch for `@deprecated` markers — they flag code being deleted.

### What this PR does

Before this PR:

Reasoning-effort control lived in three divergent sources of truth: UI
heuristic tables in the renderer, a ~20-provider `if/else` injection
tower in `src/main/ai`, and the registry descriptor — with all family
knowledge (which models reason, which knobs they take, wire dialects)
hardcoded as regex modules, unreachable for user-added models.

After this PR:

The `RuntimeReasoning` descriptor (a `controls` union of effort
vocabulary / thinking toggle / budget range) is the single source of
truth, end to end (11 commits):

- **Schema + data**: `controls` become first-class in
`@cherrystudio/provider-registry` (with native tiers like
`xhigh`/`max`); descriptors are populated at generation from models.dev
plus creator-declared completion rules, and stored rows re-enrich at
read time (`userOverrides`-gated) — no DB migration.
- **Serialization**: the provider-id branch tower collapses into a
format-keyed `REASONING_SERIALIZERS` catalog (11 wire dialects) + thin
wire overlays; the composer's ThinkingButton derives its options from
the descriptor.
- **Knowledge as creator data**: family knob rules
(`Creator.reasoningFamilies`) and the reasoning-membership gate
(`Creator.reasoningMembership`, replacing `inferReasoningFromModelId`)
are declared per creator and compiled into generated pattern artifacts
consumed by zero-knowledge matchers. Post-4.7 generations (gpt-5.6,
claude-sonnet-5/fable, doubao-seed-2.1, …) are covered.
- **User entry points**: the model editor gains a "reasoning controls"
section (declare knobs per custom model; the service normalizes the
PATCH and resolves the wire dialect from the serving provider), and
custom providers get a per-endpoint reasoning-dialect selector —
open-weight models' format follows the serving provider.
- **Cleanup**: renderer heuristic tables (~422 lines) and the dead
`model.ts` inference layer (~451 lines) are deleted.

Part of #16598 — the final legacy-tower deletion + API-gateway
unification is deferred one release by design.

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

Adding a reasoning model previously required hand-editing regex towers
in three places; now it is a data edit in one creator file plus `pnpm
generate`, and user-added models get the same treatment through the
editor. Behavior parity is enforced by a golden characterization matrix
(frozen before the refactor; updates require a reviewed old-vs-new diff
audit), a permanent `reasoningContract` gate, and an 869-id membership
corpus audit (0 lost, 27 gained — all deliberate extensions mirroring
knob rules the old gate made unreachable).

The following tradeoffs were made:

- Family rules carry knobs only, never a wire format — the dialect
always resolves from the serving provider (user endpoint config >
registry > endpoint-type default), which is why membership and knob
rules stay separate data sets.
- Legacy `supportedEfforts`/`thinkingTokenLimits` fields are kept and
derived from `controls` so existing consumers migrate incrementally
(deleted in the follow-up phase).

The following alternatives were considered:

- Runtime regex heuristics shared as code (rejected: identical to the v1
SSoT problem); auto-deriving membership from knob rules (rejected: broad
vocabulary rules like the `^qwen` toggle would over-claim non-reasoning
siblings).

Links to places where the discussion took place: #16598

### Breaking changes

None intended — wire payloads are golden-tested for byte parity;
new-generation coverage and the two new settings surfaces are additive.

### Special notes for your reviewer

The bulk of the diff is generated artifacts (golden catalogs under
`__tests__/goldens/`, regenerated `data/*.json` including unrelated
upstream drift, `*.gen.ts` pattern files). Review the sources instead:
`src/schemas/model.ts`, `src/creators/*.ts`,
`src/patterns/reasoning-{families,membership}.ts`,
`src/main/ai/utils/reasoningSerializers.ts`, and the two settings
surfaces. `catalog-source-sync` tests reject hand-edited or stale
generated files in CI.

### Checklist

This checklist is not enforcing, but it's a reminder of items that could
be relevant to every PR.
Approvers are expected to review this list.

- [x] Branch: This PR targets the correct branch — `main` for active
development, `v1` for v1 maintenance fixes
- [x] PR: The PR description is expressive enough and will help future
contributors
- [x] Code: [Write code that humans can
understand](https://en.wikiquote.org/wiki/Martin_Fowler#code-for-humans)
and [Keep it simple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/KISS_principle)
- [x] Refactor: You have [left the code cleaner than you found it (Boy
Scout
Rule)](https://learning.oreilly.com/library/view/97-things-every/9780596809515/ch08.html)
- [x] Upgrade: Impact of this change on upgrade flows was considered and
addressed if required
- [x] Documentation: A [user-guide update](https://docs.cherry-ai.com)
was considered and is present (link) or not required. Check this only
when the PR introduces or changes a user-facing feature or behavior.
- [x] Self-review: I have reviewed my own code (e.g., via
[`/gh-pr-review`](/.claude/skills/gh-pr-review/SKILL.md), `gh pr diff`,
or GitHub UI) before requesting review from others

### Release note

```release-note
Reasoning controls are now fully data-driven: thinking-effort options follow each model's descriptor (including gpt-5.6, claude-sonnet-5/fable, doubao-seed-2.1 and other new generations), custom models can declare their own reasoning controls (effort levels / thinking switch / thinking budget) in the model editor, and custom providers can pick a per-endpoint reasoning wire format in the request configuration drawer.
```

https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/blob/main/.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md?-->

> ### 🚨 Branch strategy — read before opening this PR
>
> The v2 refactor has merged into `main`, so **`main` is the default
branch for active development** (v1 and v2 code currently coexist there
— expect large, breaking changes).
>
> - **Active development** (features, refactors, optimizations, fixes
for the current codebase) → target **`main`** (the default base).
> - **v1 maintenance** (hotfixes and subsequent v1 releases) → branch
from and target **`v1`**, _not_ `main`.
>
> A v1 fix does **not** auto-carry to `main`: if the same bug exists on
`main`, open a separate forward-port PR targeting `main`. Before
touching subsystems being replaced, read `docs/references/data/` and
watch for `@deprecated` markers — they flag code being deleted.

### What this PR does

Before this PR:

Agent runs interrupted by runtime failures could leave task events in
`in_progress` and render a blank assistant reply. AI diagnosis
persistence also used the regular message endpoint for Agent-session
messages.

After this PR:

Interrupted Agent task events reach a terminal error state, terminal
assistant messages without displayable content show a localized
fallback, and Agent error diagnoses persist through session-scoped
message endpoints.

<!-- (optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)`
format, will close the issue(s) when PR gets merged)*: -->

Fixes #17265

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

The following tradeoffs were made:

The persistence boundary terminalizes interrupted task state, while the
renderer keeps a defensive fallback for malformed or content-free
terminal messages. Diagnosis updates are scoped by both session ID and
message ID to prevent cross-session writes.

The following alternatives were considered:

Hiding empty replies was rejected because it masks failures. Fixing only
the renderer was rejected because persisted `in_progress` task state
would still be incorrect. Reusing the regular message API was rejected
because Agent-session messages use separate storage and ownership
boundaries.

Links to places where the discussion took place: None

### Breaking changes

<!-- optional -->

If this PR introduces breaking changes, please describe the changes and
the impact on users.

None.

### Special notes for your reviewer

The underlying provider failure remains visible and is not reclassified
by this change. Verification passed with `pnpm build:check` (1,494 test
files; 17,703 tests passed, 65 skipped). ESLint reports 39 existing
warnings and zero errors.

feat(ai-provider): support Vertex AI MaaS open/partner models (#16862)

<!-- Template from
https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/blob/main/.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md?-->
<!--  Thanks for sending a pull request!  Here are some tips for you:
1. Consider creating this PR as draft:
https://github.com/CherryHQ/cherry-studio/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

> ### 🚨 Branch strategy — read before opening this PR
>
> The v2 refactor has merged into `main`, so **`main` is the default
branch for active development** (v1 and v2 code currently coexist there
— expect large, breaking changes).
>
> - **Active development** (features, refactors, optimizations, fixes
for the current codebase) → target **`main`** (the default base).
> - **v1 maintenance** (hotfixes and subsequent v1 releases) → branch
from and target **`v1`**, _not_ `main`.
>
> A v1 fix does **not** auto-carry to `main`: if the same bug exists on
`main`, open a separate forward-port PR targeting `main`. Before
touching subsystems being replaced, read `docs/references/data/` and
watch for `@deprecated` markers — they flag code being deleted.

### What this PR does

Before this PR: Vertex AI only served Gemini (`google-vertex`) and
Claude (`google-vertex-anthropic`) models; MaaS open/partner models
(Llama, DeepSeek, Qwen, GLM, Kimi, gpt-oss) either didn't appear in the
model list or were misrouted to the Gemini `generateContent` SDK and
failed.

After this PR: Vertex MaaS models are listed and usable under the
existing `vertexai` provider. A new `google-vertex-maas` provider
extension wraps the SDK's `createVertexMaas` (Vertex's OpenAI-compatible
Chat Completions endpoint, which mints its own GCP bearer token),
`buildVertexConfig` routes `{publisher}/{model}` non-Claude ids to it,
and the model listing bakes the publisher prefix into `apiModelId` so
the request carries the exact id the endpoint expects. No new UI or auth
surface — it reuses the existing Vertex `iam-gcp` service-account
configuration.

<!-- (optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)`
format, will close the issue(s) when PR gets merged)*: -->

Fixes #

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

The following tradeoffs were made: MaaS models are detected by the
`{publisher}/…` id shape (mirroring the existing `startsWith('claude')`
detection) rather than by adding a new endpoint type to the
provider-registry package, keeping the change contained to this repo
with no registry/UI changes.

The following alternatives were considered: a dedicated provider id +
settings UI for MaaS (rejected — it duplicates the existing Vertex
`iam-gcp` config), and a raw OpenAI-compatible provider pointed at the
Vertex OpenAPI URL (rejected — bearer tokens expire hourly, which the
SDK's `createVertexMaas` refresh handling solves for free).

Links to places where the discussion took place: <!-- optional: slack,
other GH issue, mailinglist, ... --> N/A

### Breaking changes

<!-- optional -->

None.

### Special notes for your reviewer

The exact MaaS id shape (`-maas` suffix / publisher prefix) is derived
from what Vertex's `publishers/{publisher}/models` listing returns; it
is covered by unit tests but has not been exercised against a live GCP
account, so a real fetch-models + inference pass is the recommended
validation (flagged with a `ponytail:` comment in `buildVertexConfig`).
Custom-host (proxy) MaaS setups are best-effort; the standard empty-host
path is correct.

### Checklist

This checklist is not enforcing, but it's a reminder of items that could
be relevant to every PR.
Approvers are expected to review this list.

fix(navigation): restore last viewed topic/session on re-entry (#17237)

### What this PR does

Before this PR:

- Re-entering Chat or Agents without an explicit topic/session selected
resumed the most-recently-updated conversation instead of the one the
user last viewed. Merely viewing a conversation does not update
`updatedAt`, so switching away and back could land on a different item.

After this PR:

- Re-entering Chat or Agents resumes the last viewed topic/session first
by reading the existing `ui.chat.last_used_topic_id` and
`ui.agent.last_used_session_id` persist caches.
- Explicit URL, tab metadata, and assistant deep-link targets keep
precedence. If the remembered topic/session was deleted or cannot be
loaded, the existing most-recently-updated fallback still applies.

<!-- (optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)`
format, will close the issue(s) when PR gets merged)*: -->

Fixes: N/A

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

Both pages already write the last-used ids, so this change adds only the
missing read path at the page-mount selection point where sidebar and
bare-route re-entry converge. The resume id is captured once at mount
because activating a topic/session rewrites the same cache.

The following tradeoffs were made:

- A stale or unavailable remembered id incurs one lookup before falling
through to the existing latest-updated behavior.

The following alternatives were considered:

- Passing the remembered id only through sidebar navigation. This would
fix one entry path while leaving other bare page mounts inconsistent.
- Retaining the separate last-active-module restoration. That scope was
removed from this PR to avoid overlapping with #16438.

fix(chat-shell): keep the right panel usable in narrow windows (#17240)

### What this PR does

Before this PR:

- In narrow (split-screen) windows the docked right panel was clamped by
`max(0px, calc(100% - 360px))`, so clicking any panel entry (topic flow,
tasks, files, status) opened it into a 0–6px sliver — the click appeared
to do nothing.
- The left resource list's auto-collapse only fired on window-resize
threshold crossings, so opening the panel (or starting the app already
narrow) never freed up space.
- The panel's resize divider committed the raw drag geometry even when
space-clamped, silently rewriting the stored width to a value the user
never saw, and its `aria-valuenow` reported the persisted width instead
of the visible one.
- Modal popups with fixed widths (e.g. the error-detail modal's
`minWidth: 600px`) overflowed windows narrower than that, clipping their
content and buttons.

After this PR:

- The docked panel width follows a yield-order policy shared by the
pane, its spacer, and the clip animation: the pane yields first (stored
→ 255px while the center keeps 360px), then the center yields (360 →
200px with the pane pinned), and below that both shrink proportionally —
neither side ever collapses to zero. At `available ≥ 615px` the widths
are point-for-point identical to the previous behavior.
- Left-list auto-collapse is a single predicted-center source (level +
hysteresis) replacing the two crossing-edge observers: it covers opening
the panel, starting up already narrow, and non-manual list opens, with
manual-expand suppression, drag exemption, a full-width-phase freeze
(host-reported, covering the maximized→closed exit), and Activity
keep-alive resync. User-initiated panel opens are signalled explicitly
so suppression only releases on real user intent.
- The divider stays visible whenever the pane is docked and supports
drag-to-close: overshooting the minimum-width line by 80px closes the
panel (mirroring the left list's drag-collapse), so narrow windows close
with a short flick while wide windows keep a deliberate
shrink-then-push-through feel. Resize commits only happen when they
change the effective display width, and the splitter reports that
effective width to assistive tech.
- `ContentPopup` clamps every caller-provided width
(`width`/`minWidth`/`maxWidth`) to `calc(100vw - 2rem)`, fixing the
error-detail modal and immunizing the other callers.
- PDF previews in `page-width` mode re-fit on container resizes, so the
list collapsing next to them no longer leaves a stale layout.

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

Narrow split-screen use made the right panel unusable ("clicked, nothing
happened"), reported from Windows split-screen usage; comparable
products keep the panel visible and squeeze the conversation column
instead.

The following tradeoffs were made:

- Side-by-side squeeze instead of an overlay: the panel never covers the
center content; in extreme widths both columns shrink proportionally
rather than refusing to open (an explicit product decision to avoid the
"clicked but nothing changed" feel).
- Predicted-center evaluation (a pure function of shell width +
persisted widths + panel state) instead of observing the live center
width: the prediction has no feedback loop through the thing it
collapses, which eliminates the collapse↔restore oscillation a
measured-width design exhibits.
- The freeze condition combines provider-synchronous flags
(`presentationMaximized`, `layoutAnimationPending`) with the
host-reported full-width phase, because the host report lands one commit
late and a maximize-click evaluation would otherwise bounce the list.

The following alternatives were considered:

- Overlay mode for narrow windows — rejected to keep the layout
non-covering.
- Keeping the crossing-edge observers and adding a third source —
rejected; the union semantics create "never restores" corners and the
old sources are strictly implied by the predicted model.
- A drag-distance threshold for drag-to-close — shipped first, then
replaced by the overshoot trigger because with the pane already at its
minimum the distance threshold required dragging across most of the pane
with zero feedback.

Links to places where the discussion took place: internal review (spec
iterated through 10 adversarial review rounds and 2 E2E rounds
before/after implementation).

### Breaking changes

None. Wide-window behavior (`available ≥ 615px`) is unchanged
point-for-point; narrow-window behavior changes are bug-fix territory
(the panel was previously invisible there).

### Special notes for your reviewer

- `paneWidthPolicy.ts` is the single source of truth for the width
formula; the CSS expression in `RightPaneHost` is its mirror and the
unit tests pin both plus the segment boundaries (455/615/stored+360) and
continuity.
- The auto-collapse state machine lives in `ChatAppShell`
(`useResourceListAutoCollapse`) with per-branch tests: level+hysteresis,
suppression priority over deferred evaluations, freeze race, trigger
re-declaration, unmount resync.
- Manual verification in a dev instance (CDP-measured): 700px window
open/close with list hand-off (281/360 split), 520px minimum window
(255/206), maximize↔docked↔direct-close with no list bouncing,
drag-to-close in both narrow and wide windows, error modal at 520px.

This checklist is not enforcing, but it's a reminder of items that could
be relevant to every PR.
Approvers are expected to review this list.

development, `v1` for v1 maintenance fixes
- [x] PR: The PR description is expressive enough and will help future

- [x] Code: [Write code that humans can
understand](https://en.wikiquote.org/wiki/Martin_Fowler#code-for-humans)
and [Keep it simple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/KISS_principle)
- [x] Refactor: You have [left the code cleaner than you found it (Boy
Scout
Rule)](https://learning.oreilly.com/library/view/97-things-every/9780596809515/ch08.html)
- [x] Upgrade: Impact of this change on upgrade flows was considered and
addressed if required
- [x] Documentation: A [user-guide update](https://docs.cherry-ai.com)
was considered and is present (link) or not required. Check this only

```release-note
Fixed the right panel being invisible when opened in narrow (split-screen) windows: the panel and the conversation column now share space gracefully, the left list yields automatically when space runs out, the divider supports drag-to-close, and fixed-width modals no longer overflow narrow windows.
```

<!-- (optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)`
format, will close the issue(s) when PR gets merged)*: -->

Fixes # None

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

The root search previously indexed descendant text into the parent
launcher, so a child match could only return the parent. Dynamic Skill
rows were also owned by their custom panel and had no root-search
representation. This change gives the unified panel search-only
actionable rows while preserving the normal hierarchical menu when no
query is present.

The following tradeoffs were made:

- Search-only rows are included in the root panel's backing list and
filtered out for an empty query.
- Custom panels must explicitly opt actionable rows into root search;
this keeps read-only status panels out.

The following alternatives were considered:

- Flattening every child row was rejected because MCP contains read-only
status rows rather than selectable actions.
- Keeping parent-only matches was rejected because it still requires an
extra navigation step after a specific child match.

### What this PR does

Before this PR:

- The agent edit dialog only exposed installed skill toggles.
- Agent creation exposed global uninstall actions that did not belong in
the form flow.
- Toggling a skill replaced the cached list with a loading placeholder
during background revalidation, causing a visible flash.

After this PR:

- Agent creation and editing share the same skill search, add, and
select-all controls.
- Agent forms no longer expose skill uninstall actions.
- Cached skills stay visible while background revalidation completes.

<!-- (optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)`
format, will close the issue(s) when PR gets merged)*: -->

Fixes # N/A

### Why we need it and why it was done in this way

The shared `SkillCatalogPicker` already owns the complete agent
skill-selection experience, so the edit dialog now reuses it instead of
maintaining a reduced duplicate. Initial loading remains distinct from
background refresh so cached content is not unmounted during autosave.

The following tradeoffs were made:

- Cached skill data remains visible briefly while the server-backed
enablement projection revalidates.

The following alternatives were considered:

- Adding separate search and import controls to the edit dialog was
rejected because it would duplicate the create flow.
- Keeping background refresh classified as loading was rejected because
it caused the visible list flash.
