feat(chat): render immersive HTML previews inline (#17158) > ### 🚨 Branch strategy — read before opening this PR > > The v2 refactor has merged into `main`, so **`main` is the default branch for active development** (v1 and v2 code currently coexist there — expect large, breaking changes). > > - **Active development** (features, refactors, optimizations, fixes for the current codebase) → target **`main`** (the default base). > - **v1 maintenance** (hotfixes and subsequent v1 releases) → branch from and target **`v1`**, _not_ `main`. > > A v1 fix does **not** auto-carry to `main`: if the same bug exists on `main`, open a separate forward-port PR targeting `main`. Before touching subsystems being replaced, read `docs/references/data/` and watch for `@deprecated` markers — they flag code being deleted. ### What this PR does Before this PR: - Assistant HTML fences used the legacy artifact card and moved previewing away from the code block's original Markdown position. - The v2 Markdown path no longer recognized top-level raw HTML written directly in an assistant message as an HTML artifact, regressing the v1 behavior unless the model wrapped the HTML in a fenced code block. - Inline previews used a fixed 16:9 desktop canvas instead of fitting the generated document. After this PR: - Assistant HTML artifacts render as immersive inline previews at their original message position, with a streaming placeholder, zoom controls, and read-only source/preview switching. - **This intentionally restores the v1 design:** top-level raw HTML authored directly in Markdown, without a fenced code block, is routed through the same HTML artifact preview as fenced HTML. - Formatted full HTML documents remain one artifact even when the Markdown parser emits the doctype, head, style, and body as separate top-level HTML nodes. - The preview adapts to the rendered document height and available conversation width, capped at 72% of the conversation viewport. - A completed preview waits for the smooth-stream tail to reach the final source before mounting its iframe, avoiding repeated post-completion reloads. - Automatically mounted previews do not grant script or form permissions. The existing restricted CSP is applied as defense in depth, not as a new complete network-isolation boundary. Fixes # N/A ### Why we need it and why it was done in this way Restoring direct Markdown HTML previewing preserves v1 behavior and avoids making preview availability depend on whether the model emits a fenced code block. Keeping the preview inside the assistant response also preserves conversational context and makes generated pages immediately inspectable without opening a separate pane. The implementation adds a remark transform only when inline HTML previewing is enabled. It routes top-level raw HTML AST nodes into the existing HTML artifact renderer and coalesces a complete `...` document when remark splits it across adjacent nodes. The renderer reuses the existing `HtmlPreviewFrame` path instead of inserting generated page markup into the Markdown layout. The following tradeoffs were made: - Raw HTML artifact detection is limited to top-level HTML regions. Inline HTML inside Markdown paragraphs remains in the normal Markdown path so text formatting and citations keep their existing behavior. - Only complete HTML documents are coalesced; separate standalone HTML regions remain separate artifacts. - SVG remains in its dedicated Markdown renderer, while standalone comments and doctypes do not create previews. - Preview height follows rendered iframe content but is capped at 72% of the conversation viewport; taller documents scroll within the preview. - Streaming keeps the placeholder visible until smoothed content reaches the final source. This can delay the first iframe mount briefly, but prevents incomplete-document reloads after completion. - The source view is intentionally read-only. Editing and saving changes back into a message are outside this PR's preview scope. - The auto-mounted path disables scripts and forms while retaining `allow-same-origin` only for parent-side content measurement. - Full HTML sandbox redesign, CSP-injection hardening, and browser request-layer filtering are intentionally outside this feature PR. They require an independent threat model, design, and dedicated PR. The following alternatives were considered: - Supporting fenced HTML only was rejected because it preserves the v2 regression and makes the result depend on model formatting. - Rendering raw HTML directly inside the Markdown tree was rejected because it mixes generated page layout with message layout instead of using the existing artifact preview path. - Reusing the legacy `HtmlArtifactsCard` was rejected because it keeps previewing outside the Markdown flow. - Adding an editable source view was not included because this PR does not define a persistence contract for modifying assistant messages. - Expanding this PR into a general security-boundary redesign was rejected because the shared `HtmlPreviewFrame` CSP injection and outbound-request policy predate this feature and should be addressed coherently in a separate PR. Links to places where the discussion took place: None ### Breaking changes None. ### Special notes for your reviewer - This is an intentional restoration of v1 user-facing behavior in the v2 Markdown path, not a new general-purpose raw HTML renderer. - Both fenced HTML and top-level raw HTML use the same artifact preview; complete documents are coalesced, while inline paragraph HTML and SVG retain their existing renderers. - Source inspection is read-only. Existing external-open and HTML download actions remain available. - The automatically mounted path no longer grants `allow-scripts` or `allow-forms`; `allow-same-origin` remains only so the parent can measure preview height. - The existing restricted CSP is reused as defense in depth. This PR does not claim or implement complete network isolation for generated HTML. - Further sandbox, CSP-injection, or request-layer security hardening must be designed, reviewed, and tested in a separate PR rather than expanding this feature PR. - Regression tests were added for the smooth-stream tail, complete-document grouping, and the inline preview's script/form restriction. - `pnpm format` and `pnpm lint` pass. Lint reports 39 pre-existing warnings unrelated to this change; its type checks and i18n check pass. - `pnpm test`, `pnpm build:check`, and manual UI verification of the latest fix were not run under the workspace verification policy. ### Checklist This checklist is not enforcing, but it's a reminder of items that could be relevant to every PR. Approvers are expected to review this list. - [x] Branch: This PR targets the correct branch — `main` for active development, `v1` for v1 maintenance fixes - [x] PR: The PR description is expressive enough and will help future contributors - [x] Code: [Write code that humans can understand](https://en.wikiquote.org/wiki/Martin_Fowler#code-for-humans) and [Keep it simple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/KISS_principle) - [x] Refactor: You have [left the code cleaner than you found it (Boy Scout Rule)](https://learning.oreilly.com/library/view/97-things-every/9780596809515/ch08.html) - [x] Upgrade: Impact of this change on upgrade flows was considered and addressed if required - [x] Documentation: A [user-guide update](https://docs.cherry-ai.com) was considered and is present (link) or not required. Check this only when the PR introduces or changes a user-facing feature or behavior. - [x] Self-review: I have reviewed my own code (e.g., via [`/gh-pr-review`](/.claude/skills/gh-pr-review/SKILL.md), `gh pr diff`, or GitHub UI) before requesting review from others ### Release note ```release-note Assistant messages once again render top-level raw HTML as inline previews. Complete HTML documents stay in one preview, and completed previews no longer reload while the final streamed text is draining. ``` --------- Signed-off-by: jd <59188306+zhangjiadi225@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: SuYao

commits.message:
MOD-13409 Harden RDB loading (#1038)

* MOD-13409 Validate Bloom RDB bitset size on load (#126)

* MOD-13409 Validate Bloom RDB bitset size on load

Reject crafted module payloads where the loaded bloom bitset buffer length does not match the declared bits/n2, and add a flow regression test covering RESTORE with a corrupted DUMP payload.

* MOD-13409 clang-format rebloom.c

Run clang-format on rebloom.c to satisfy make lint formatting checks.

* MOD-13409 Validate Bloom RDB bitset length on load

Reject crafted module payloads where persisted bits do not match the loaded bitset buffer length, preventing OOB access after RESTORE.

* Mod 13409 rdb hardening (#127)

* MOD-13409 clang-format rebloom.c

Run clang-format on rebloom.c to satisfy make lint formatting checks.

* MOD-13409 Harden CF RDB load buffer validation

Replace assert-only checks in CFRdbLoad with explicit validation of loaded sizes and ranges, and add a flow test that corrupts a CF DUMP payload to ensure RESTORE is rejected.

* MOD-13409 Harden remaining RDB loaders (TDigest)

TDigest RDB loading trusted attacker-controlled cap, which could desync the allocated centroid arrays from the loaded metadata and enable OOB access after RESTORE.

Reject payloads where loaded cap differs from the allocated cap derived from compression, validate node counters, and add a flow regression that corrupts a TDigest DUMP payload and asserts RESTORE is rejected.

With the earlier BF/CF fixes, this covers all remaining module data structures that load raw buffers from RDB/RESTORE (CMS/TopK already validate buffer sizes).

* Revert "MOD-13409 clang-format rebloom.c"

This reverts commit 456e53503f51b68c4d7a9df988e50a36adf2e61a.

* MOD-13409 Add regression for published P88W exploit

* MOD-13409 Share RDB corruption test helpers

* Fix TDigest RDB load node count overflow check

Avoid signed overflow when validating merged and unmerged node counts by widening the addition.

fix(cli): show per-file progress at default verbosity (#3477)

* fix(cli): show per-file progress at default verbosity

Without -v, the CLI sets root logging to WARNING, which silences the
"Processing document <name>" line emitted by the pipeline at INFO. On
multi-file inputs (e.g. a directory of audio files) the user has no
indication of which file is currently in flight.

Bump docling.pipeline.base_pipeline and docling.document_converter to
INFO at default verbosity so per-file progress remains visible while
keeping noisier model-loading logs hidden.

Resolves #3467

Signed-off-by: Truffle <truffleagent@gmail.com>

* feat(cli): add -q/--quiet to restore fully silent output

Showing per-file progress at default verbosity is a behavior change for
callers that shell out to docling and expect silence unless a warning or
error occurs (AI agents especially, where unexpected INFO lines bloat the
context window).

Add a -q/--quiet flag that opts back out: at default verbosity it leaves
the progress loggers at WARNING, restoring the previous fully silent
default. The flag has no effect when -v/--verbose is given. Mention the
option in the app help preamble so `docling --help` surfaces it to agents.

Signed-off-by: Truffle <truffleagent@gmail.com>

---------

commits.message:
Preserve Claude feature flags in interactive clients (#1239) ## Problem `fcc-claude` and the documented IDE setups forced `DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1`. Claude Code disables feature-flag fetching with that variable, so rollout features such as fullscreen rendering and mouse support fell back to disabled defaults. Fixes #1238. ## Changes | Before | After | | --- | --- | | Shared proxy policy forced telemetry off for interactive and managed Claude sessions. | Interactive Claude sessions preserve the user's telemetry choice, while managed headless tasks own the opt-out. | | VS Code and JetBrains examples disabled Claude feature-flag fetching. | Both IDE examples allow Claude to fetch rollout feature flags. | | Environment contracts expected every Claude surface to disable telemetry. | Regression tests distinguish interactive inheritance from managed enforcement. | | Package metadata reported version 4.12.0. | Package metadata and the lockfile report version 4.12.1. |

Greptile Summary

 Preserve users' telemetry preferences in interactive Claude clients while continuing to disable telemetry in managed headless sessions. - Removes the shared `DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1` override and the equivalent IDE setup examples. - Adds the telemetry opt-out specifically to managed Claude environments. - Updates regression tests to distinguish interactive inheritance from managed enforcement. - Bumps package and lockfile metadata from 4.12.0 to 4.12.1.

Confidence Score: 5/5

The PR appears safe to merge, with interactive inheritance and managed telemetry enforcement consistently implemented and tested. The interactive launcher now passes through the parent telemetry preference, while the only managed subprocess path applies its telemetry opt-out after constructing the shared environment and passes that environment directly to process creation. No files require additional attention.

T-Rex Logs

 **What T-Rex did** - Compared the pre-change HEAD^ state to the new HEAD, confirming DISABLE\_TELEMETRY handling now preserves inherited values, omits when not inherited, forces 1 for managed environments and invocations, and removes the traffic-suppression flag, resulting in all six checks passing with exit code 0. Ran code and verified through T-Rex

Important Files Changed

 | Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | src/free_claude_code/cli/claude_env.py | Removes the shared telemetry override so interactive Claude launches inherit the parent environment. | | src/free_claude_code/cli/managed/claude.py | Restores the telemetry opt-out specifically for managed headless Claude subprocesses. | | tests/cli/test_entrypoints.py | Verifies that interactive clients preserve an explicit telemetry setting and leave an absent setting unset. | | tests/cli/test_managed_claude.py | Verifies that managed sessions enforce the noninteractive telemetry policy. | | README.md | Removes telemetry-disabling configuration from the documented VS Code and JetBrains interactive setups. | | pyproject.toml | Bumps the project version to 4.12.1. | | uv.lock | Synchronizes the editable project package version without changing dependency resolutions. |

Flowchart

 ```mermaid %%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%% flowchart TD Base[Parent environment] --> Shared[build_claude_proxy_env] Shared --> Interactive[Interactive Claude client] Shared --> Managed[build_managed_claude_env] Interactive --> Preserve[Preserve user's DISABLE_TELEMETRY choice] Managed --> Disable[Set DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1] Disable --> Headless[Managed headless Claude process] ```
Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["fix: preserve Claude interactive feature..."](https://github.com/alishahryar1/free-claude-code/commit/cddcf0d61639a33aba46cac3f7bcbe0274cbcc60) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=46750932)

commits.message:
ci(nightly): give the Windows Multiple Daemons example more build headroom (#2742) (#2812)

ci(nightly): give the Windows Multiple Daemons example more build headroom

`cargo run --example multiple-daemons` triggers `dora build`, which recompiles
the node/operator crates (zenoh, arrow, rustls, quinn) from scratch in release.
On a cold Windows runner that can exceed the 30-min step budget — the
2026-07-23 nightly was force-killed mid-zenoh-compile. Raise the step to 45 min
and the job cap 90->120 so the from-scratch build sequence fits without masking
a genuine hang.

Refs #2742

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(core): keep a file:// drive letter out of the git clone cache path (#2742) (#2811)

fix(core): keep a file:// drive letter out of the git clone cache path

`clone_dir_path` built the on-disk cache path by extending it with the repo
URL's raw path segments. A `file:///C:/...` mirror URL parses with the drive
letter `C:` as its first segment, and the colon makes that an unnameable
directory on Windows, so the daemon's clone destination could not be created
and `dora build` against a local `file://` mirror failed there.

#2795's new `reuse_still_verifies_a_dir_left_by_a_different_finished_session`
test is the first to actually clone from a `file://` URL, so it surfaced this
latent bug (present since #2043) on the nightly Windows runner.

Sanitize each host/path component, replacing the Windows-reserved characters
with `_`. It is a no-op for normal `https://host/owner/repo` URLs, so existing
caches are untouched. Adds a platform-independent regression test asserting no
emitted component keeps a colon.

Refs #2742

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.message: feat: deny the LSP tool when launching Claude Code to keep the prefix cache stable

fix(memory-guard): drain the pressure reclaim at the step boundary under load (#2342)

Completes #2306 for busy servers: under sustained load, hard memory pressure livelocked because the idle-gated reclaim never fired while requests were running. Adds a pressure-reclaim flag drained at the step boundary via the same guarded _sync_and_clear_cache path, returning pooled Metal buffers to the OS even mid-decode.

Fixes #2059
feat: support MXFP8 source models in oQ
fix: keep the sdpa256 unfused fast path when the memory guard is disabled (#2283)

feat(sdpa256): log the first tiled-route engagement per reason (#2352)

The head-dim-256 prefill router silently chooses between the fast unfused
pass and the ~2x-slower O(L)-memory tiled pass. Nothing surfaced the
decision, so a user on the tiled path at long context sees only the
throughput loss. Log the first engagement per reason (forced env /
no headroom provider / no active ceiling / insufficient headroom with the
MiB numbers / probe error) at INFO, keeping repeats silent so the hot
path stays quiet. The probe-error branch also gains a DEBUG with the
traceback it previously swallowed.

Refs: 2283
fix: enforce Q8 for GLM DSA indexers (#2347)

fix: decode JSON-quoted string values in XML tool call coercion

#2332 added an early return for string-typed params in _coerce_param_value so plain values like 42 or {"a": 1} for a string param would not get coerced to a non-string. That also stopped JSON-quoted string literals from being decoded, so a MiniMax-style value like "SF" leaked its surrounding quotes into the tool arguments as 'city': '"SF"'. Now a value that is a valid JSON string literal is decoded back to the bare string, while plain values stay verbatim. This restores the pre-#2332 round-trip for the namespaced (MiniMax) parser and unbreaks the two streaming regression tests.
fix: schema-aware param coercion in XML tool call fallbacks (#2332)

Add missing Russian macOS localizations (#2329)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@example.invalid>
fix: keep prefix cache reconstruction on the per-engine stream (#2330)
chore: bump version to 0.5.3

Add Baidu Unlimited-OCR support and oQ fixes (#2328)

Adds support for baidu/Unlimited-OCR (a DeepSeek-V2 + SAM-ViT-B + CLIP-L document OCR model) so it loads, serves, and quantizes end to end. Closes #2314.

The model implementation comes from mlx-vlm PR Blaizzy/mlx-vlm#1427 by @shuuul (MIT), vendored under omlx.patches.mlx_vlm_unlimited_ocr_compat since it landed after our pinned mlx-vlm commit rather than bumping the whole pin. Full credit for the model, the processor, and the R-SWA language wrapper goes to that PR.

- Vendor the unlimited_ocr package and register the model_type for discovery, the OCR prompt, and generation defaults, reusing the deepseekocr SAM and CLIP encoder already present at the pin so no pin bump is needed.
- Fall back to mlx-vlm's get_chat_template plain rendering when a VLM ships no chat template, fixing the HTTP 400 the raw Unlimited-OCR checkpoint hit in the token-count preflight and vision-input prep.
- Fix three oQ bugs so the DeepSeek-OCR family quantizes into a loadable checkpoint: detect VLMs by the vision sub-config predicate, dispatch Model.sanitize by argument count for the staticmethod variant, and skip the sam_model SAM encoder whose neck and net convs are nn.Conv2d that mlx cannot quantize.

fix: infer GLM MTP quant specs from tensor shapes when config has none (#2326)

The Alis 3.5bpw checkpoint packs the nextn layer at 3-bit but records no per-module quantization entry for layer 78 at all (only its own mtp.layer.* naming that nothing maps), so the earlier checkpoint-key remap had nothing to copy and the MTP block was still built at the global 4-bit width. Sanitize now recovers each remapped mtp.* module's true bits and group_size from the packed/scales shapes plus the unquantized module input dim (shapes alone are ambiguous between gs32/6-bit and gs64/3-bit) and publishes them to args.quantization, which is the same dict the load-time class predicate reads.
fix(admin): retry HF browse anonymously on stale token 401 and round search card headers (#2310)
fix(admin): rebuild tailwind css for log level button styles

fix: name the tripped abort threshold in memory abort message (#2321)

The client-facing abort message printed the ceiling, so a hard-pressure abort read as "usage 28.8 GB, ceiling 30.2 GB" with usage below the printed limit. Propagate the hard watermark (ceiling x hard_threshold) to the scheduler each tick and print it as the abort threshold, and align the advice tail with the preflight rejection wording.

fix: build GLM native MTP module with per-module quant overrides (#2326)

The sanitize patch remaps the nextn layer weights to mtp.<i>.* but the per-module entries in config quantization stayed keyed by the checkpoint path, so mlx-lm's class predicate missed them and built the MTP block at the global bit width. from_dict now copies the overrides to the runtime paths. The admin MTP toggle check also switches to the loader's _checkpoint_has_mtp_weights so native nextn layouts are recognized.

fix(admin): drain HF model list in worker thread to keep event loop free (#2325)

huggingface_hub list_models returns a lazy generator, so asyncio.to_thread only wrapped the generator creation and the actual paginated HTTP ran on the event loop during iteration. When huggingface.co is unreachable the whole server blocked, health checks failed, and the app marked the server unresponsive. Draining the generator inside the worker thread keeps the loop free and makes the 10s timeout actually apply.
ci: ignore dependabot xgrammar bumps while transformers cap conflicts

revert: pin xgrammar back to 0.2.3 to unbreak dev installs

Xgrammar 0.2.4 added a transformers<5 cap that conflicts with our transformers>=5.7.0 pin, so the exact dev pin from #2289 made pip and uv dev installs unresolvable. 0.2.5 keeps the same cap, so hold at 0.2.3 until upstream lifts it. Stub targets and the build.py fallback move back together to keep the sync test green.
fix(admin): highlight included levels in log viewer filter
fix(mac): skip CLI shell path prompt when rc block already installed (#2320)

fix: load language_model-prefixed laguna checkpoints on the text path

Community conversions and oQ outputs produced through the mlx-vlm route nest every key under language_model. (e.g. mlx-community/Laguna-S-2.1-oQ4e, which has no vision_config and classifies as llm). Strip the prefix in sanitize so strict load_weights matches the flat text tree.

Add native Laguna S-2.1 support (#2322)

* feat: add native Laguna MLX-LM support

(cherry picked from commit 3ebd6ac417b0d8309d7cc8bf0dcf3e01fa429428)

* chore: keep agent discovery guide local

(cherry picked from commit 9d38f15701da8d5aff7bf8d72a634f085fc22353)

* refactor: drop server-side laguna enable_thinking override

Laguna S-2.1 ships a chat template with enable_thinking | default(true), so the request-time override from #2223 is no longer needed. Discovery-level detection stays.

* feat: extend laguna patch for S-2.1 architecture

Honor mlp_layer_types and gating_types per-layer lists, anchor the enable_thinking | default(true) template heuristic, pin the laguna tool parser so mlx-lm template sniffing does not pick glm47, and restore the upstream arg-pair regex that tolerates literal \n between tags.

* feat: load Laguna FP8 and INT4 compressed-tensors checkpoints

Stack experts before format transforms, decode e4m3 block weights via mx.from_fp8 and requantize to 8-bit affine, unpack symmetric int4 pairs without relying on weight_shape, and normalize laguna compressed-tensors configs so mlx-lm's legacy int4 assumption is bypassed.

* feat: repack Laguna compressed nvfp4 checkpoints to mlx nvfp4

Reinterpret fp4 codes bit-exactly into the uint32 packing and fold weight_global_scale into the e4m3 group scales, mirroring the mlx-vlm #1650 approach without importing its privates.

* perf: enable MoE gate+up fusion for the vendored laguna model

* feat: accept Laguna FP8 and bf16 checkpoints as oQ/oQe sources

Register the vendored laguna class during sanitizer builds, allow compressed-tensors float-quantized sources in validate_quantizable, and pair X.weight_scale block scales in the lazy index so FP8 shards stream-dequantize instead of tripping the non-replayable sanitize path.

---------

Co-authored-by: jundot <jundot@users.noreply.github.com>

commits.message:
Merge pull request #133 from dannymcc/fix/workout-max-hr-spike

fix(workout): suppress PPG spikes in the workout max HR (#127)

Merge pull request #135 from dannymcc/fix/briefing-time-of-day

fix(briefing): greet for the actual time of day, not the period

Merge pull request #137 from dannymcc/fix/notification-dedupe

fix(notify): enforce fire-once dedupe for insight notifications

Merge pull request #126 from dannymcc/fix/ring-phone-auto-stop

fix(actions): stop 'ring my phone' ringing until force-close

test(notify): make concurrency tests deterministic via an entry signal

The concurrent-emit tests used Future.delayed(Duration.zero) to assume the
first emit had reached the fake sink before releasing the gate — a
scheduler-dependent barrier that could flake. The gated sink now completes
an `entered` Completer the moment the first emit reaches the parked point,
and the tests await that signal instead, so the overlap is guaranteed by
ordering rather than timing. Assertions are unchanged.

fix(notify): serialise emit's dedupe check-present-record section

Two overlapping emit() calls could both pass FiredKeyStore.hasFired before
either recorded the key (→ both present the same alert), and their
read-modify-write of the shared_preferences fired-key list could clobber
each other (losing a key). This is more likely now the UI-thread
stress-screen alert can race the background derive loop.

Guard the hasFired → present → recordFired section with a small in-memory
chained-Future lock so overlapping emits run one at a time; recordFired
re-reads the latest list inside the serialised section, so no stale
read-modify-write. Dart's single isolate makes this sufficient — no deps.

Adds concurrency tests: overlapping emits of the same key present exactly
once; overlapping emits of different keys both record (neither clobbered).

fix(notify): route high-stress alert through emit for gating + dedupe

The stress screen called NotificationService.presentEvent directly for its
"High Stress Detected" alert, bypassing both the prefs gate (category /
quiet-hours) and the new fire-once dedupe guard — so it could re-alert on
repeat screen visits and ignore the user's quiet hours. Route it through
NotificationCenter.emit like every other insight, so its
"$date:high_stress" key inherits the same gating and fires at most once.

Event fields are unchanged (health category, normal priority, no route);
the call stays fire-and-forget (nothing read its return value).

fix(notify): enforce fire-once dedupe for insight notifications

Insight/health notifications carry a dedupeKey ("$date:$kind") and their
call sites promise "fires at most once/day", but nothing enforced it. The
OS notification id derived from the key only REPLACES a prior post in the
shade — it still re-alerts — and derivation re-runs on every BLE sync, so
any insight whose condition holds all day (e.g. today's irregular-rhythm
flag) re-fired the notification over and over (#136). Illness, anomaly,
temperature, readiness, HR-shift, recovery-ready, step-goal and
auto-workout alerts all shared this latent bug.

Add a persistent, bounded fired-key guard (FiredKeyStore, backed by
shared_preferences): NotificationCenter.emit now skips a dedupeKey that
has already fired and records it only after a real present, so a
permission-denied no-op doesn't consume the key. The guard resets itself
per new day via the date-prefixed keys and is capped to the most-recent
200 keys (oldest evicted) so it stays bounded without ever letting a
same-day key re-fire.

Paths that SHOULD repeat are untouched: device battery/charging alerts
(showDevice, edge-triggered arm/re-arm) and scheduled water/wind-down
reminders (zonedSchedule) never route through emit.

Closes #136

fix(briefing): capture one effective timestamp per generation

Reuse a single now for the day label, greeting and generatedAt so a null-now
generation can't straddle midnight or a greeting boundary. Addresses CodeRabbit
review on #135.

fix(briefing): greet for the actual time of day, not the period

The morning briefing (last night's sleep + recovery) is shown until 17:00, so
the prompt telling the model it's the 'morning' briefing made the AI greet with
'morning' in the afternoon (#134). Pass the reader's real local time of day into
the prompt and greet for that; keep the morning/evening period only as the data
scope.

fix(workout): suppress PPG dropouts in the workout min HR (#127)

Symmetric to the max fix: a single 1-2 s low PPG dropout could define
the workout min via the raw `reduce(math.min)`. Apply the same rolling
median + physiological reject to the trough.

- hr_max.dart: factor the max helper into a shared core and add
  smoothedMinHr / smoothedMinHrAt (rolling median over the same window so
  a 1-2 s low can't be the median; the floor/ceiling reject drops garbage
  such as a stray sub-30 bpm reading before the min).
- getWorkout: min_hr now uses smoothedMinHr instead of reduce(math.min).
- getWorkouts list: fill min_hr from the batched raw via smoothedMinHr so
  the list agrees with the detail recompute; the SQL MIN(d.hr) is now
  floor-bounded (minHrFloor) and used only when raw is pruned.

The live path tracks no running min, so no accumulator was added there.
Keep the HR-page path untouched.

Extend the tests: a 1-2 s low dropout is excluded, a genuine sustained
low is preserved, the physiological floor drops sub-30 garbage, and the
repo-seam fixture now carries a 40 bpm dropout + 20 bpm garbage that
neither getWorkout nor getWorkouts lets define the min.

fix(plan): guide model to continue planning on non-Yes plan_exit/plan_enter answers (#1883)

* fix: guide model to continue planning when plan_exit/plan_enter is answered No

Answering No previously threw QuestionRejectedError, which hard-stopped
the turn and left the model with an intent-free 'dismissed' error next
turn — causing it to go silent or start implementing while still in plan
mode (#1812). Now No and custom-feedback answers resolve normally with
explicit stay-in-mode guidance, and every plan->plan turn gets a short
plan-mode reminder.

* docs: finalize plan-no-continue spec (delivered)

* fix: dedup plan-mode reminder across multi-step turns

insertReminders runs on every loop step and persists parts, so the
plan->plan reminder (and the entry reminder once the new branch made
step 2+ reachable) would stack duplicates into the user message —
growing the DB and invalidating the prompt prefix cache on every step.
Guard on an existing 'Plan mode is' part. Spec updated with dedup
rationale and upgrade/prefix-cache compatibility notes.

* refactor: simplify plan reminder dedup to positional fresh-turn guard

Replace content matching on 'Plan mode is' with messages.at(-1) ===
userMessage — inject only at step 1 of a fresh user turn, which encodes
the intended semantic directly and also covers entry-turn step 2+.

feat: Add anonymous analytics via GoatCounter

playground: anonymous, cookieless visit stats via GoatCounter

One async script tag plus a thin wrapper (js/analytics.js) that queues
events until count.js arrives and silently no-ops when it's blocked or
down — the playground never depends on it. Counts pageviews and a small
set of per-visit events: tutorial started/completed per scenario, tasks
resolved, side quests tried, TUI opened, install modal/copy, scenario
switches, and outbound repo clicks. A footer note discloses the stats
and links to the public dashboard; docs/README.md documents the setup.

playground: mobile menu, stacked modals, pannable TUI; live CPU values

Mobile-only layout changes (desktop is unchanged — the menu wrapper renders as
display:contents with explicit flex order reproducing the original button
row exactly):

- Collapse the topbar into a ☰ menu (TUI / Scenario / Reset / Install / Star);
  only the theme toggle and the Tutorial/Free-play button stay outside it. The
  topbar gets a z-index so the dropdown isn't painted under later content (its

  modal cards scroll when taller than the screen.

  instead of cramming its panes; the disclaimer stays pinned while panning.

Also give every process an idle-realistic cpuPercent (mirroring the memory
injection) so top and the TUI's CPU% column read like a live box instead of
0.0 everywhere — the runaway ffmpeg still clearly dominates. Consumed only by
the sim's top/TUI, so golden fixtures are untouched (16/16).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01K4HgTJBEvAQBjstvdUcrYQ

playground: give every process realistic memory data

The TUI's Mem column, its memory-descending default sort, top's %MEM, and
--verbose all consume per-process memory — but only 2 of 42 processes carried
it, so most rows read "0 B" and the sort was degenerate. Inject plausible
vms/rss/shared for every process in both worlds (nginx workers ~17 MB, dockerd
~90 MB, VS Code renderer ~340 MB, the runaway ffmpeg ~470 MB, and so on),
sized against each box's memTotalBytes.

The zombie (pid 6120) intentionally keeps no memory — a defunct process has
already released it, so 0 B there is the authentic reading.

Golden fixtures regenerated (postgres verbose now includes its Memory section,
rendered by witr's real output package); JS engine parity still 16/16.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fresh-eyes walkthrough findings:

- The devbox CPU task says "top shows pid 6001 pegged" — but typing top
  returned "command not found". Add a static top-style snapshot (sorted by CPU
  then memory, hot processes in red, world-driven) so the runaway ffmpeg is
  discoverable rather than asserted; htop points to top. Listed in help and
  tab completion.
- The suggested-command chips still pitched the old story (kill 8123,
  witr ffmpeg). Realign them with the current tasks per scenario.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

playground: fix uneven install-modal columns; reorder Linux/Windows methods

The install cards looked lopsided because grid 1fr (= minmax(auto,1fr)) let the
long winget command stretch its column wider than the other, overflowing the
card. Use minmax(0,1fr) + min-width:0 so commands ellipsize and both columns
stay equal.

Also reorder methods by what most users want first: Windows leads with winget
(script second); Linux shows APT last since the apt package lags releases.

playground: fix constellation flicker, colorize TUI detail, user-driven first task

- Constellation now renders on demand instead of every frame. The old loop
  lerped the camera toward its target forever (never settling) while the scene
  auto-rotated, so anti-aliased lines/points resampled by sub-pixel amounts each
  frame — that shimmer was the "flicker". The camera now eases to its target and

  read on its dark surface (the page's light-theme ANSI palette was too dark).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01K4HgTJBEvAQBjstvdUcrYQ

playground: TUI fidelity, informational webbox, authentic warnings, polish

Close the remaining gaps against the real witr TUI and rework the webbox
tutorial around witr's "just answer the why" strength.

TUI (match the real bubbletea dashboard):
- Descriptive per-tab search placeholders; Mem column shows "rss (pct%)";
  processes default-sort by memory desc with a "Mem ↓" indicator (Ports "Port ↑",
  Containers "Name ↑").

  beside the environment, with ↓/↑/↕ scroll indicators on pane headers.
- Ports gains the "Attached Processes" side pane; Containers gains the Command
  column.

Incident content:
- webbox is now three informational investigations that resolve the moment
  witr answers them — the :8000 blocker (free the port or use another), the apt
  lock (the scheduled unattended-upgrade you forgot), and a --verbose footprint
  check on the app. No forced kills.
- devbox briefing now flows from the git-lock cold open; the scenario itself is
  unchanged.

Authenticity & UX:
- Warnings are now the strings witr actually emits ("Process is listening on a
  public interface", "…high CPU (>2h total)", "…zombie (defunct)"); fabricated
  ones removed. Golden fixtures regenerated.
- Light-theme is the default already; the TUI keeps its own terminal palette and
  now carries a "simulated recreation, real witr may differ" disclaimer (also in
  the free-play welcome).
- Cold open no longer shows a duplicate prompt (the live line hides while
  locked). Constellation reframes to the panel so it isn't bunched up, stops the
  resize flicker, and a click on empty space returns to the full view.
- Capitalized Incident/Resolved/All clear; scenario-aware free-play hints; a
  "witr -h" reference in the finale; and the next action button flashes so it's
  clear where to go.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01K4HgTJBEvAQBjstvdUcrYQ

playground: light-first theme, gentler incident, faithful TUI, and polish

A batch of playground UX changes:

- Theme: default to light and only switch to dark when the user toggles it —
  no automatic switch based on the OS colour-scheme preference.
- Cold open: speed up the deploy.sh build sequence ~1.5x.
- After the opening `witr --port 8000`, rewind the terminal to the top so the
  reader can take the story in from the beginning. Terminal now sticks to the
  bottom only while already near it (and disables scroll-anchoring), so later
  auto-resolve notes don't yank a reader who has scrolled up.
- webbox incident reworked so the intro isn't a scary kill: the :8000 blocker
  is your own orphaned instance that clears itself, the apt lock resolves on
  its own, and the ngrok tunnel is the single "stop it by hand" action. Fix
  buttons can carry a friendlier label (e.g. "Close the tunnel"). devbox's
  git-lock is reframed the same way.
- TUI rewritten to match the real bubbletea dashboard: purple brand badge and
  green/grey numbered tabs, the actual columns (PID/User/Name/CPU%/Mem/Started/
  Command), Ports/Containers/Locks tables, the live "Details" ancestry pane,
  the real status + footer text with version, and a proper Process Detail view
  (standard witr output beside the environment) with the a: Actions menu
  (kill/term/pause/resume/nice) and confirm prompts. A kill from the TUI
  removes the process everywhere and updates the incident tracker.
- Leaving the tutorial now clears the screen and drops a short welcome; the
  topbar button reflects the mode ("Tutorial" ⇄ "Free play"). Rename the
  "Dashboard" button to "TUI".
- Add breathing room above the "Explore freely" actions.
- Spread the constellation nodes further apart (wider leaf spacing, smaller
  node/halo) so busy sibling clusters don't bunch up.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01K4HgTJBEvAQBjstvdUcrYQ

playground: run witr's real engine in-browser via WebAssembly

Phase 2 — the playground can now run witr's actual Go engine, compiled to
js/wasm, alongside the JS simulation. An "Engine" toggle in the header switches
between them; the real engine lazy-loads (~4 MB) only when turned on.

The browser becomes a fifth witr platform:
- internal/proc/world_js.go serves process/port/lock data from the in-memory
  world instead of /proc; internal/source/detect_js.go injects the
  systemd/launchd/bsd-rc unit metadata a browser can't introspect;
  internal/target/resolve_js.go resolves name/port/file targets.
- cmd/witr-wasm is the WASM entry point, exposing witrRun(worldJSON, nowMs, argv).
- Everything else — ancestry walking, container/SSH/shell/supervisor detection,
  warning generation, and all output rendering — is witr's real code, unchanged.

As a result the real engine produces genuinely more complete output than the
simulation: real "running as root" / public-interface warnings, real supervisor
detection, the real container fallback, and real exit codes.

Supporting changes:
- sudo_user_unix.go now excludes js (the WASM data provider supplies the
  commandAsOriginalUser symbol); linux/darwin/freebsd/windows builds unchanged.
- Reconcile the node source with real witr behaviour (systemd unit +
  NRestarts rather than an ad-hoc restart field) so both engines agree;
  fixtures regenerated, JS fidelity check still 16/16.
- build-wasm.sh builds witr.wasm and vendors wasm_exec.js (binary gitignored);
  check-wasm.mjs smoke-tests the WASM engine (7 commands).
- CI builds and smoke-tests the WASM engine and ships it in the Pages artifact.

A zero-install, in-browser playground for witr, deployable to GitHub Pages.
It runs real witr commands against a simulated Linux box and works both as a
guided tutorial and as a free-form sandbox.

- Dependency-free terminal widget with history and tab-completion; light/dark
  theme; mobile layout with tappable command chips.

- README: prominent "Try witr in your browser" link.

commits.message:
chore: daily docs link check workflow (#2059)

fixes #2036

This PR reworks the Lychee automation workflow:

* Replaces the old source-file link checker with a daily workflow that
builds the MkDocs site and checks rendered HTML
* Adds a two-pass confirmation step (90s wait + cache clear + re-run) to
reduce false positives before failing or opening issues
* On confirmed failure (scheduled runs only): opens/reuses a Link
Checker Report issue
* On success (scheduled runs only): auto-closes any open link checker
issue
* Expands `lychee.toml` with timeouts, retries, caching, and excludes
for sites that block or flake in CI
* Fixes several broken doc links found during testing

commits.message:
docs(base): clarify complete and partial updates (#1993)

* docs(base): clarify complete and partial updates

Consolidate the update rule introduced in #1879 and make the command-contract boundary explicit. Full-update commands must use trusted current configuration for the first actual request, while delta commands should send the smallest legal payload.

* docs(base): clarify full-update state preservation

Address review feedback by requiring unchanged writable configuration to remain intact, except when the requested update makes a setting inapplicable.

* docs(base): strengthen update contract guidance

feat(slides): fill xml-schema-quick-ref gaps that forced XSD fallback (#2026)

From EVAL-07-22-02-53 (42 convos), agents fell back to the full XSD for:
- shape type enum + presetHandlers (rounded corners)
- polyline (bounding-box positioning, required border, connector type)
- table merged cells (colspan / rowspan)

Add compact coverage for each, sized to real usage (shape/polyline type
lists trimmed to what actually appears in generations). Chart gaps deferred.

fix/task id handling (#2023)

* fix: validate task GUID inputs

* fix: make task updates self-confirming

* fix: confirm task completion state

* docs: clarify task ID workflow

* test: cover task ID dry runs

* fix: address task ID review feedback
fix(slides): normalize presentation flag aliases (#2032)
docs(skill): clarify scope handling for query expansion (#2030)
feat(apps): support absolute and relative upload paths (#2005)

feat(apps): validate +file-list --page-size against server (0, 200] range (#2007)

paas_storage AppFileListForOpenAPI rejects page_size > 200 at the inner
checkMaxKeys guard with ErrInvalidRequest("maxKeys not in range (0, 200]").
Previously the CLI forwarded any --page-size straight to the API, so
--page-size 500 produced an opaque server error round-trip.

Add a client-side Validate check bounding --page-size to [1, 200] (aligned
with the existing validateAppsPageSize precedent in the observability
commands): out-of-range values now fail fast with a typed validation error
and never hit the network. The server tolerates page_size <= 0 by defaulting
to 20, but the CLI default is already 20 and an explicit < 1 is a user error,
so we reject it for a clearer message, consistent with other list commands.

Update the flag description and the lark-apps-file skill reference to
document the 1..200 range, and cover the boundaries in unit tests.

feat: introducing official card icon (#1973)

Card header icon documentation contained invalid tokens (e.g., mail_colorful, approve_colorful) that do not render, and icon guidance lacked precise token enumeration, causing LLM to guess or fabricate icon tokens. This PR replaces
examples with valid tokens and adds a definitive colorful icon reference table.

repos.name: HeyWillow/willow
repos.description: Open source, local, and self-hosted Amazon Echo/Google Home competitive Voice Assistant alternative

commits.message:
ethernet: enable SNTP and timezone

The Ethernet path skipped the shared SNTP lifecycle, so it neither
applied the configured timezone nor synchronized the system clock.

Expose the helpers and initialize SNTP before bringing up the W5500
interface. Start it after the existing connection wait. The shared
configuration already selects the Ethernet IP event.

ethernet: set MAC-derived hostname

Pass the concrete esp-netif handle to the shared hostname helper instead
of selecting the first registered interface. This preserves Wi-Fi
behavior and lets Ethernet set its MAC-derived hostname before DHCP
starts.

Store the Ethernet interface in hdl_netif so the remaining WAS code can
read the configured hostname.

ethernet: derive MAC address from ESP

The W5500 has no burned factory MAC address, so assigning a fixed test
address makes every Willow Ethernet interface collide on the same LAN.

Read ESP_MAC_ETH from ESP-IDF and assign that derived address to the
W5500 driver. Remove the unused fake address left in network.c.

ethernet: adapt W5500 setup to the current ESP-IDF API

ESP-IDF changed ETH_W5500_DEFAULT_CONFIG() to accept the SPI host
and device configuration. The W5500 driver now allocates the SPI
device internally.

Stop creating the SPI device explicitly and pass the host and device
configuration directly to the W5500 configuration macro. This fixes
the Ethernet build with the current ESP-IDF version.

main: always declare sz

Similar to the previous commit, we need to always declare the sz
variable, as we need it to get the WAS URL from NVS.

main: always declare hdl_nvs

Always declare hdl_nvs regardless of Ethernet or Wi-Fi, as we need it to
read the WAS URL if we use Ethernet.

Fixes the following error when CONFIG_WILLOW_ETHERNET is enabled:

/willow/main/main.c: In function 'app_main':
/willow/main/main.c:125:42: error: 'hdl_nvs' undeclared (first use in this function)
  125 |     err = nvs_open("WAS", NVS_READONLY, &hdl_nvs);
      |                                          ^~~~~~~

ethernet: reuse the default event loop

init_system() creates the default ESP-IDF event loop before network
initialization. Creating it again in init_ethernet() returns
ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE and makes ESP_ERROR_CHECK abort Ethernet startup.

Reuse the system-owned event loop, matching the Wi-Fi path and keeping
Ethernet initialization focused on its driver and event handlers.

build: expose ESP-IDF Ethernet configuration

Add esp_eth as an unconditional private component requirement so
ESP-IDF loads its Kconfig before evaluating Willow's Ethernet
option. Without it, ETH_SPI_ETHERNET_W5500 is undefined and
CONFIG_WILLOW_ETHERNET cannot be enabled.

ESP-IDF expands component dependencies before loading configuration.
The esp_eth component remains configuration-only when
CONFIG_ETH_ENABLED is disabled, so Wi-Fi builds do not compile the
Ethernet driver.

repos.description: Pure python3 implementation for working with iDevices (iPhone, etc...).

commits.message:
Merge pull request #1801 from doronz88/chore/report-deprecated

typing: enforce reportDeprecated and fix the 7 flagged sites

typing: enforce reportDeprecated and fix the 7 flagged sites

Promotes reportDeprecated to "error" in [tool.pyright] (applies to tests too —
catching deprecated API use is as valuable there). Clears the 7 existing hits:

- mobile_activation: logging.Logger.warn() -> warning() (warn is deprecated).
- Six @contextmanager/@asynccontextmanager helpers annotated their generator
  functions as Iterator[T]/AsyncIterator[T]; typeshed deprecates that overload in
  favour of the accurate Generator[T, None, None]/AsyncGenerator[T, None] (these
  functions genuinely are generators). Uses the 3.9-safe multi-arg forms.
  webinspector.get_js_completions stays AsyncIterator[...] — it is a plain async
  iterator, not a context manager, so it was correctly not flagged.

Annotation-only apart from the warn->warning rename; base pyright 0, cli green.

Merge pull request #1800 from doronz88/chore/proper-package

packaging: adopt PEP 621 inline deps and ship py.typed (PEP 561)

packaging: adopt PEP 621 inline deps and ship py.typed (PEP 561)

Two packaging-hygiene changes that turn this into a properly-declared,
type-exporting distribution now that the package is pyright-clean end to end.

py.typed (PEP 561): add the marker (and a package-data entry so it ships in the
wheel). Without it, a type checker in a consumer's project ignores all of our
inline annotations and treats every pymobiledevice3 symbol as Any — the marker
is what makes the typing work visible downstream. Verified present in the built
wheel at pymobiledevice3/py.typed.

Inline dependencies (PEP 621): move the runtime requirements from requirements.txt
into [project.dependencies] so pyproject.toml is the single source of truth,
dropping the setuptools dynamic-dependencies indirection (version stays dynamic
via setuptools_scm). Pins and environment markers are unchanged; the built
wheel's Requires-Dist is identical to before. requirements.txt is removed, and
its now-stale references are cleaned up: the path filters / cache-dependency
globs in the python-app and codeql workflows (installs already use
`pip install -e ".[test]"`, i.e. the project metadata) and a doc comment in
userspace_tunnel.py.

No code behaviour change. Base pyright 0, cli tests green, wheel builds.

Merge pull request #1798 from doronz88/feature/report-unknown

typing: clear reportUnknown* package-wide and enforce it

typing: clear reportUnknown* package-wide and enforce it

Completes the third and final bucket of the post-`standard` typing campaign
(after reportMissingTypeArgument and reportMissingParameterType): eliminates
every reportUnknown{Variable,Member,Argument,Parameter,Lambda}Type diagnostic
from the package (~1504 -> 0) and promotes the five rules to "error" in
[tool.pyright] so regressions fail CI. tests/ is exempted via the existing
executionEnvironments block (fixtures/mocks are deliberately loosely typed, same
rationale as reportMissingParameterType).

All fixes are annotation/cast-only and behaviour-preserving:
- bare []/{}/set() collections annotated at their declaration;
- values/members from untyped third-party libraries (pyusb, opack2, qh3,
  srptools, pygments, inquirer3, xonsh, coloredlogs, hexdump, pcapng,
  pyiosbackup, parameter_decorators, ipsw_parser BuildIdentity, win32security,
  PyAV, Pillow) routed through cast(Any, ...) on the expression or a
  TYPE_CHECKING signature stub;
- construct/DTX dynamic payloads carried as dict[str, Any];
- cast(Any, module).attr for lazy/untyped module attributes (note: a
  getattr(mod, "const") workaround is rewritten back to attribute access by ruff
  B009, so the module-cast form is the only ruff-stable one);
- LocationSimulation.__init__ calls super().__init__(dvt) so the generic _SVC_T
  binds (identical runtime: DtxService.__init__ does not chain super()).

hevc_av/cdp_screencast route PyAV and Pillow access through Any so they stay
reportUnknown-clean regardless of installed stubs: CI resolves PyAV as fully
unknown (optional dep, reportMissingImports ignored) and ships a looser
Image.resize stub, while local envs type both — the leak was otherwise invisible
until the pyproject flip.

Also drop unnecessary string quotes from ~50 cast() type arguments: cast()'s
first argument is evaluated as a runtime expression, so a string literal is only
warranted when evaluating the type would fail (TYPE_CHECKING-only name, forward
ref, or non-subscriptable type). Left quoted where required: irecv
cast("array[int]", ...) (array.array is not subscriptable before 3.11) and
dtx/primitives cast("Context", ...) (TYPE_CHECKING-only construct import).

Merge pull request #1799 from doronz88/bugfix/irecv-dispose-on-reinit

irecv: dispose the USB handle in _reinit to fix restore hang after reset

irecv: dispose the USB handle in _reinit to fix restore hang after reset

Fixes a regression introduced in v9.40.0 where a device restore hangs at
"If the restore hangs here, disconnect & reconnect the device": _find() spins
forever and never re-acquires the device after reset() ("no device in the
desired mode"), while a fresh re-run of the restore succeeds.

Root cause: v9.40.0's typing cleanup introduced local captures like
`device = self.device` in send_buffer(). send_buffer() calls self.reset() ->
_reinit() while that local is still live, so setting `self._device = None` no
longer drops the last reference — the usb.core.Device is not garbage-collected
and its libusb handle stays claimed. The reset device then can't re-enumerate
cleanly and the subsequent _find() reads a stale device / loops.

_reinit() now calls usb.util.dispose_resources() on the outgoing device before
dropping it, releasing the handle explicitly instead of relying on refcount/GC.

Merge pull request #1797 from doronz88/feature/param-types-slice-1

typing: annotate all unannotated params + enforce reportMissingParameterType globally

typing: finish reportMissingParameterType — cycle 3 + enforce globally

Annotates the remaining ~291 unannotated params across the long tail (dvt
instruments, core_device/remote media & transport, restore, dtx, services, cli,
osu, __main__, utils, …), completing the reportMissingParameterType campaign
(694 total). Flips the rule on globally in pyproject.toml and removes the 20
per-file `# pyright: reportMissingParameterType=error` headers from earlier
slices. tests/ is exempted (test fixtures/monkeypatch/mock stubs are
deliberately loose) via an executionEnvironments override.

Notable correctness fixes surfaced by the typing:
  * dtx/service.py: the dtx_method / dtx_on_invoke / dtx_on_data / _on_notification
    / _on_dispatch decorators are now typed with an identity TypeVar + overloads
    (bare and arg forms) so `self.service.<method>(...)` stays callable/typed —
    a naive `fn: Any` had collapsed every DVT service method to a non-callable
    object (52 cascade errors across device_info, dtx_services, etc.).
  * SupportError/FeatureNotSupportedError os_name -> Optional[str] (callers pass
    the possibly-None _os_name).

Inferred types throughout; genuinely-dynamic DTX/ctypes callback params stay Any.
Annotation-only aside from the two fixes above. pyright 0, ruff clean, cli tests pass.

typing: param annotations for the rest of web_protocol (cycle 2b)

Re-does the six web_protocol files whose work was discarded by a parallel
agent's stray git-stash in cycle 2a (cdp_target, selenium_api, driver,
session_protocol, switch_to, alert; ~116 params), and adds the
`# pyright: reportMissingParameterType=error` header to all 14 cycle-2 files.

Package-wide reportMissingParameterType now 492 -> 291.

Real bug fixed along the way: typing `session: AutomationSession` in
switch_to.new_window surfaced a missing await — create_window() (a coroutine)
was passed straight into switch_to_window() instead of a resolved window handle.
Added the await.

Annotation-only otherwise; inferred types (CDP payloads dict[str, Any], selector
strings, By/Optional[str], session_protocol TYPE_CHECKING ids). pyright 0, ruff
clean, cli tests pass.

typing: param annotations for web_protocol survivors (cycle 2a)

Partial cycle-2 batch (automation_session, element, cdp_server, cdp_screencast,
inspector_session, webinspector, cli/webinspector, wda). Split out to protect it
after a parallel agent's stray git-stash discarded the sibling files' work;
those are redone in the following commit. Directives added with the remainder.

typing: annotate unannotated params (slice 1 of reportMissingParameterType)

First slice of the reportMissingParameterType campaign (implicit-Any params).
Annotates ~202 params across 6 core files — dtx/primitives.py, services/afc.py,
irecv.py, restore/tss.py, lockdown.py, remote/xpc_message.py — bringing the
package-wide count 694 -> 492.

Types are inferred from body usage and callers, precise where determinable:
construct signatures (stream: IO[bytes], context: Context, path: str) in the
codec methods; str paths / bytes payloads / int handles in AFC and IRecv;
Container[Any] for XPC decoder inputs; canonical __aexit__ /__exit__ exception
triples (+ from types import TracebackType); dict[str, Any] for dynamically-keyed
plist request/response params. Genuinely-dynamic params (xonsh internals, RPC
dispatch) stay Any.

img4_create_local_manifest's build_identity is typed Mapping[str, Any] (not
dict) so it accepts the BuildIdentity UserDict the recovery path passes.

Enforced per-file via a  header
(removed + flipped global at campaign end). Annotation-only.

Merge pull request #1796 from doronz88/feature/construct-typed-tunnel

tunnel: type CDTunnelPacket / RPPairingPacket with construct_typed

tunnel: type CDTunnelPacket / RPPairingPacket with construct_typed

Convert the two CoreDevice tunnel wire packets from plain construct Struct to
construct_typed.DataclassStruct backed by CDTunnelPacketData /
RPPairingPacketData. CDTunnelPacket.parse(...).body is now typed bytes instead
of Container/Any at the three CDTunnel receive sites; build sites pass a
dataclass instance instead of a dict (incl. the pairing-host test simulator).

The const `magic` fields use the shared construct_compat.const_field() helper.

Wire format unchanged — verified byte-identical build (CDTunnel\x00\x07…,
RPPairing\x00\x07…) and clean parse round-trips on BOTH Python 3.14/
construct-typing 0.8.0 and Python 3.9/construct-typing 0.7.0.

Merge pull request #1795 from doronz88/feature/construct-typed-dtx

construct: typed DTX wire structs + shared construct-typing compat helper

construct: extract the csfield_const version shim into construct_compat

The construct-typing 0.7.x (Python 3.9) vs >= 0.8.0 shim for const struct fields
was about to be duplicated in every module that defines a Const-bearing
DataclassStruct. Extract it into pymobiledevice3/construct_compat.const_field()
and use it from the new DTX structs and from afc.py (dropping afc's local
try/except copy).

const_field() picks csfield_const() on >= 0.8.0 and csfield(Const(...)) on 0.7.x.
Verified on both toolchains: byte-identical builds and clean round-trips for the
DTX and AFC headers under Python 3.14/construct-typing 0.8.0 and Python
3.9/construct-typing 0.7.0.

dtx: type the DTX wire structs with construct_typed (DataclassStruct)

Convert the two core DTX wire structs (dtx_fragment_header,
dtx_fragment_payload_header) from plain construct `Struct(...)` — whose
`.parse()` returns a dynamically-typed `Container` (field access is `Any`) — to
`construct_typed.DataclassStruct` backed by typed `DtxFragmentHeader` /
`DtxFragmentPayloadHeader` dataclasses. Parse results now carry precise `int`
field types instead of `Any`, at the fragment/message hot paths.

Build call sites pass a dataclass instance instead of a dict. Wire format is
unchanged — verified byte-identical build and clean parse round-trips.

The const `magic` field uses the same construct-typing version shim as afc.py:
csfield_const() on >= 0.8.0, csfield(Const(...)) on 0.7.x (the Python 3.9 floor
that lacks csfield_const).

First step of migrating plain Struct usages to the typed API to eliminate
construct-sourced `Any`.

Merge pull request #1794 from doronz88/feature/type-args-final

typing: enforce reportMissingTypeArgument globally (final slice)

typing: enforce reportMissingTypeArgument globally (final slice)

Completes the reportMissingTypeArgument campaign. Clears the remaining ~158
bare-generic sites across the long tail (73 package modules + 6 test files),
removes the per-file `# pyright: reportMissingTypeArgument=error` headers added
by slices 1-3, and flips the rule on package-wide in pyproject.toml.

The whole package + tests are now free of unparametrized generics; every
`dict`/`list`/`tuple`/`set`/`Task`/`Queue`/`Future`/`Callable`/`Pattern`/... in
an annotation carries type arguments.

Same policy as prior slices: heterogeneous plist/DTX/XPC payloads -> dict[str,
Any]; precise types where evident, e.g. Task[None] for None-returning reader
loops, Future[DTXMessage], Queue[Optional[bytes]], Queue[tuple[bytes, Any]],
Pattern[str], IO[bytes], PromptSession[str], list[bytes], tuple[str, int].

Two runtime-safety notes handled: construct's Construct/Adapter are not
PEP-585-subscriptable, so those use a TYPE_CHECKING alias; tqdm has no
__class_getitem__, so its annotation is quoted (get_type_hints-safe on 3.9).

Annotation-only — no runtime behaviour changes.

Each cleaned file carries a `# pyright: reportMissingTypeArgument=error` header;

typing: parametrize bare dict/list generics (slice 1 of reportMissingTypeArgument)

commits.message:
fix(vscode): bound brace-expansion resolution to avoid poisoned 5.x (#467)

The resolutions entry "brace-expansion": ">=2.1.2" had no upper bound,
so yarn resolved it to the poisoned 5.0.7 release whose changed export
shape breaks minimatch's default import, crashing `yarn lint` with
"brace_expansion_1.default is not a function".

Bound the range to ">=2.1.2 <3" (keeping the CVE-safe lower bound from
#445) and regenerate yarn.lock. brace-expansion now resolves to 2.1.2
and its transitive balanced-match back to 1.0.2. Lint, compile and the
92 unit tests all pass.
feat(viewer): fix top breadcrumb to get more comfortable experience (#298)

chore(deps): let dependabot cover the VS Code extension (#437)

dependabot.yml declares gomod and github-actions only. The repo has 9
npm manifests, and extensions/vscode/package.json:110-116 is currently
carrying five hand-written CVE resolutions pins - undici, form-data,
js-yaml, minimatch - i.e. someone is patching CVEs by hand in a package
dependabot never looks at.

Scope is deliberately just /extensions/vscode: its yarn.lock is tracked
(Yarn Classic v1, which the npm ecosystem supports). /pages is excluded
because its lockfile is gitignored (.gitignore:13) - a maintainer policy
call, not mine. / and the six npm/*/ stubs have no real dependencies.

@types/vscode is ignored because package.json pins it to ^1.74.0 to
match engines.vscode ^1.74.0; bumping the types alone would type-check
against APIs absent from the declared minimum VS Code. Grouped to
minor/patch so the breaking majors already pending (eslint 8->9,
@typescript-eslint 6->8, @types/node 18->24, vsce ->3) arrive
individually rather than as one unreviewable PR.

ci(translation-sync): remove redundant push trigger (#460)

* ci(translation-sync): remove redundant push trigger

PR validation already covers translation sync before merge; re-running
on the post-merge push to main wastes CI.

Signed-off-by: shaurya2k06 <shaurya2k06@gmail.com>

* Made Requested Changes

Signed-off-by: shaurya2k06 <shaurya2k06@gmail.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: shaurya2k06 <shaurya2k06@gmail.com>

ci: add translation-sync guardrails for READMEs and docs (#455)

* ci: add translation-sync guardrails for READMEs and docs

Add a check-translation-sync script + tests wired into CI: a blocking
check that all five README.<locale>.md files share an identical level-2
heading structure (compared by structure not translated text, and
code-fence-aware), and a non-blocking warning when a docs/en page changes
without its zh/ja counterparts. Addresses #419.

* ci: address review feedback on translation-sync guardrails

- Move the translation-sync job out of ci.yml into its own
  .github/workflows/translation-sync.yml, scoped with paths: filters
  (README*.md, pages/src/content/docs/**, the checker scripts) so it
  only runs on translation changes and never blocks the core pipeline.
- runReadmeCheck: short-circuit with exit 1 when any expected README*.md
  is missing, before the structure comparison, so a missing file can no
  longer mask a real divergence.
- extractHeadings: add a TODO(commonmark) note that closing fences are
  matched by fence char only, not by length.
- test: drop the dead en/zh fixtures and the void statements in
  testReorderedHeadingFails; keep the en2/zh2 pair that actually drives
  the assertion.

* ci: fix length-unaware code-fence parsing and pin node image

Address the automated review comments beyond the maintainer's minor note:

- extractHeadings: track the opening fence length and only close on a
  same-char run that is at least as long (per CommonMark). Previously a
  block opened with ```` and containing an inner ``` closed early, so
  lines inside the block could be misread as headings (or real headings
  dropped), producing false-positive structure-divergence errors. Adds a
  regression test covering the inner-shorter-fence case.
- translation-sync.yml: pin node:24.18.0 instead of the mutable node:24
  major tag, matching the golang:1.26.5 pin used by the other jobs.

fix(deps): upgrade grpc-go to v1.82.1 for GHSA-hrxh-6v49-42gf (#456)

Fixes Dependabot alert #26 (High severity). Addresses xDS RBAC
authorization bypass, HTTP/2 Rapid Reset DoS bypass, and xDS RBAC
engine panic vulnerabilities.

commits.message:
Merge pull request #297 from igoropaniuk/fix/provision-sector-probe

firehose: do not probe sector size when storage init is skipped

Merge pull request #289 from igoropaniuk/refactor/edl-interface-identity

qdl: drop the EDL product id allowlist and match the EDL interface signature when enumerating

firehose: do not probe sector size when storage init is skipped

firehose_try_configure() discovers the storage sector size by reading
sector 1 of partition 0 with each candidate size. The provisioning
path also runs this probe, and on a device with unprovisioned UFS the
reads fail inside the programmer with "OPEN handle NULL and no error,
weird": there is no LU 0 to open. The programmer does not recover
from that failed open - the subsequent provisioning commit fails with
"ufs_open(0, WLUN_DEV) failed for configure" and the device is left
unprovisioned, while the same programmer, device and XML provision
fine through QFIL, which sends no reads before the ufs tags.

Gate the probe on skip_storage_init: the probe reads force the
programmer to initialize the very storage the caller declared
uninitialized, and provisioning - the only path passing
skip_storage_init - expresses UFS geometry in kilobytes and never
needs a sector size. Reported in the github issue [1].

[1] https://github.com/linux-msm/qdl/issues/294

Signed-off-by: Igor Opaniuk <igor.opaniuk@oss.qualcomm.com>

commits.message:
[trainer] fix: isolate TaskRunner logs from root logger changes (#7133)

### What does this PR do?

This PR prevents `verl.*` INFO and WARNING logs in `TaskRunnerV1` from
disappearing after vLLM server initialization. The vLLM OpenAI/SageMaker
reconfigures the process root logger and its existing handlers to
`ERROR` by default. Since verl loggers previously propagated to root,
subsequent trainer logs were filtered out.
The change configures a dedicated `verl` namespace handler at the
beginning of `TaskRunnerV1.run()`, after Ray redirects worker stderr and
before trainer/vLLM imports. It respects `VERL_LOGGING_LEVEL` and
disables propagation from the `verl` namespace to root.

### Test

E2E separate async tested. Logs now appear.

[trainer] feat: V1 replay buffer eviction/refill handling for stale, DAPO-filtered, and failed rollout groups (#7082)

### What does this PR do?

1. Unify V1 ReplayBuffer eviction/refill handling for stale,
DAPO-filtered, and failed rollout groups.
2. Add DAPO group filtering for V1 trainers with mode-specific refill
behavior.
3. Prevent late trajectory writes by publishing terminal prompt status
only after all sessions settle.
4. Aggregate eviction metrics across polling iterations and
parameter-sync substeps, including a WandB table for filtered
reward-value counts.
5. ReplayBuffer and ReplayBufferAsync are separated into two classes.

Here is the evict-and-refill matrix, in which ``k`` is the number of
prompts to evict:
```
    |   trainer mode   |   drop   |               DAPO               |  failure |
    | ---------------- | -------- | -------------------------------- | -------- |
    |       sync       |   NO-OP  |    Evict ``k``; refill ``2k``    |   NO-OP  |
    |      async       |       All the same: Evict ``k``; refill ``k``.         |
```

``drop`` means off-policy staleness dropping, which is only for async
trainers.
``DAPO`` means filtering groups whose configured reward metric is
identical across all trajectories.
``failure`` is the group status described above.
Validation treats all terminal groups as sampleable.

In sync mode, DAPO is opt-in and trades generation time for training
stability; refilling ``2k`` and dispatching them under a bounded
in-flight limit balances retry rounds against over-generation. Failure
groups retain the standard sync behavior and remain sampleable, avoiding
refill-induced tail latency by default.
In async mode, ``num_warmup_batches`` absorbs retry cost, so all three
paths refill exactly ``k`` prompts.
``algorithm.filter_groups.max_num_gen_batches`` is intentionally
ignored; refill continues until enough groups
are sampleable.


### API and Usage Example

Set `trainer.v1.sampler.max_off_policy_strategy=drop` to drop stale
samples older than `trainer.v1.sampler.max_off_policy_threshold`

Set 
```
algorithm.filter_groups.enable=True \
algorithm.filter_groups.metric=acc \
algorithm.filter_groups.max_inflight_gen_batches=1

```
(modify metric name if you define your own AgentLoop) to use dapo
dynamic sampling.

[rollout, hardware] fix: fix rollout replica.py for device abstraction (#7128)

### What does this PR do?

Replace the hardcoded `"cuda"/"npu"` device selection in RolloutReplica
with
`get_device_name()` (→ `get_platform().device_name`,
verl/utils/device.py#L95).

The old expression `"cuda" if not is_torch_npu_available() else "npu"`
can only
emit "cuda" or "npu", so any other accelerator — e.g. Intel XPU — is
silently
mislabeled "cuda". `get_device_name()` resolves via the platform
registry, so
CUDA/NPU behave exactly as before (superset) and XPU now resolves
correctly.

Both replica init paths are fixed:
init_colocated()
[replica.py](https://github.com/verl-project/verl/blob/main/verl/workers/rollout/replica.py#L184)
(reward-model / GRM loop)
init_standalone()
[replica.py](https://github.com/verl-project/verl/blob/main/verl/workers/rollout/replica.py#L223)(main_generation_server
standalone path)

Repro (XPU):
  └─ python3 -m verl.trainer.main_generation_server
       └─ start_server() → RolloutReplica.init_standalone()
            └─ RayWorkerGroup(device_name=…)  ← previously "cuda" on XPU

[doc] chore: warn against hard-wrapping PR body markdown in pr skill (#7116)

### What does this PR do?

The `pr` skill (`.agent/skills/pr/SKILL.md`, also surfaced via the
`.claude` and `.codex` symlinks) tells an agent how to create or update
a PR, but it never said how to format the PR body. As a result, bodies
were sometimes hand-wrapped at a fixed column width. GitHub renders a
single newline inside a paragraph or list item as a space, so those
manual wraps become unintended mid-sentence breaks in the rendered PR.

This adds one paragraph to step 2 ("Compose PR Title and Body")
instructing the agent to write the body as GitHub-flavored Markdown: one
logical line per paragraph and list item, blocks separated by blank
lines, and no column-limit hard wrapping. It also calls out two
easy-to-miss cases and adds a pre-submit re-read check.

### Checklist Before Starting

- [x] Search for similar PRs: no open PR edits
`.agent/skills/pr/SKILL.md` or documents PR-body Markdown formatting.
- [x] Format the PR title as `[{modules}] {type}: {description}`: `[doc]
chore: warn against hard-wrapping PR body markdown in pr skill`.

### Test

Documentation-only change to a skill instruction file. No code paths are
affected.

`pre-commit run --all-files --show-diff-on-failure` passes (ruff,
ruff-format, mypy, generated-config, docs/license/naming/structure
checks, compileall).

### API and Usage Example

No API or configuration change. The skill now guides body composition:

- one logical line per paragraph and per list item;
- blank lines between blocks;
- no hard wrapping at a column limit (a single newline renders as a
space);
- watch continuation lines that start with `#<number>` (they read as a
heading in source) and indented list-item continuations;
- re-read the body before submitting to confirm nothing is split across
source lines.

### Design & Code Changes

- Append one guidance paragraph to the "Compose PR Title and Body"
section of `.agent/skills/pr/SKILL.md`. The `.claude/skills/pr` and
`.codex/skills/pr` entries are symlinks to the same file, so all three
stay in sync.

### AI assistance

AI assistance was used to draft this change. The human submitter
reviewed the wording and confirmed the pre-commit result before
submission.

repos.name: databendlabs/openraft
repos.description: rust raft with improvements

commits.message:
feat: add heartbeat_min_interval to suppress redundant heartbeats

# Summary

Add a `heartbeat_min_interval` config option that suppresses the
periodic heartbeat to a follower that has recently acknowledged an
RPC. A replication response proves the same liveness facts as a
heartbeat response and already updates the leader lease, so the
heartbeat is redundant. Defaults to 0, which keeps current behavior.

# Details

The heartbeat broadcast in RaftCore now skips a follower whose last
acknowledged RPC (replication or heartbeat) was sent within
`heartbeat_min_interval` milliseconds, decided by the new
`Leader::need_heartbeat()` against `clock_progress`, which records the
sending time of the last acknowledged RPC. Under sustained writes this
eliminates most heartbeat RPCs; heartbeats resume automatically once
replication idles.

Config validation enforces
`heartbeat_interval + heartbeat_min_interval < election_timeout_min`
via the new `ConfigError::HeartbeatMinIntervalTooLarge`, so
suppression can never delay a heartbeat past a follower's election
timeout.

- Part: #1853

commits.message:
Use checkout v7 for Wine job

Fixes a warning about using Node.js v20.

Fix semicolon_in_expressions_from_macros warning

Latest nightly emits a warning about "trailing semicolon in macro used
in expression position", with the following output

    error: trailing semicolon in macro used in expression position
       --> src/macros.rs:93:11
        |
     93 |           { log::$level!($($t)*) }
        |             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
        |
       ::: src/poll.rs:580:9
        |
    580 | /         trace!(
    581 | |             "registering event source with poller: token={:?}, interests={:?}",
    582 | |             token,
    583 | |             interests
    584 | |         );
        | |_________- in this macro invocation
        |
        = note: macro invocations at the end of a block are treated as expressions
        = note: to ignore the value produced by the macro, add a semicolon after the invocation of `trace`
        = warning: this was previously accepted by the compiler but is being phased out; it will become a hard error in a future release!
        = note: for more information, see issue #79813 <https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/79813>
        = note: `#[deny(semicolon_in_expressions_from_macros)]` (part of `#[deny(future_incompatible)]`) on by default
        = note: this error originates in the macro `log::trace` which comes from the expansion of the macro `trace` (in Nightly builds, run with -Z macro-backtrace for more info)

The linked issue and description are a little misleading because they
mostly talk about having a semicolon and removing it to fix the warning.
We have to do the opposite here though.
